The trees themselves have changed. Their bark has crystallized into glass-like spines—sharp, brittle, and everywhere. Walking through the Jungle of Spines means constant small cuts unless you move with extreme care. These wounds would be merely painful in any normal jungle. Here, they can be fatal.

**Spine Sickness**: Wounds from the crystalline spines carry traces of the petrification curse. Left untreated, the flesh around the wound begins to harden over the following days. The process is slow—a week or more before it becomes dangerous—but inexorable. The hardening spreads from the wound site, eventually reaching vital organs. Death comes when the heart turns to stone.

The people of Attla know remedies: poultices made from certain eastern plants, rituals involving salt water and moonlight, careful excision of affected tissue. These treatments work if applied early. Visitors to Phyr Island are advised to either avoid the Jungle of Spines entirely or travel with someone who knows the cures.

The jungle's wildlife has adapted in various ways. Some creatures have developed immunity to the spine sickness. Others have become carriers—their bites or scratches transmit the curse more efficiently than the spines themselves. A few have evolved partial petrification as a defense mechanism, their hides studded with stone growths that turn aside predators' teeth.

name: Jungles of Godahi
content:
Tags: jungle, region, geography

The Jungles of Godahi form the vast interior basin of the northern Emerald Coast—a sea of green canopy stretching roughly 300 miles east to west and 200 miles north to south. This is the heart of jungle civilization on Rimihuica's eastern seaboard, home to five distinct nations that have carved domains from the endless green.

name: Jungles of Titania
content:
Tags: jungle, biome, heartland

The green heart of Gorath. The Jungles of Titania sprawl across central Gorath between the Lucenia and Droaz rivers, stretching from the Myjornis Mountains in the north to the coastal lowlands in the south. This is where Gorath was born, where the empire's wealth grows, and where most of its million citizens live.

Despite centuries of settlement, the jungle remains dominant. Gorathi civilization exists in clearings carved from the green—along rivers, around fortress-cities, in the logged-over hills—while the forest surrounds everything, always pushing back, always ready to reclaim what has been taken. This is not a tamed land. It is a land under perpetual siege from the vegetation, and the Gorathi are winning only because they never stop fighting.

name: Jungula Serpentine
content:
The northwestern peninsula, a finger of jungle extending toward the Sea of Seven Snakes. Named for the serpents that infest its waterways—some venomous, some constrictors large enough to take a man, and rumors of something larger still in the deep interior.

Vassum nominally controls Jungula Serpentine, but "control" means they patrol the coast and maintain a few logging camps. The interior belongs to the snakes and whatever else lives there. Pirates from the Sea of Seven Snakes sometimes use the peninsula's hidden coves as anchorages, and Vassum's navy—such as it is—plays an eternal game of cat and mouse with smugglers running contraband through the serpent-infested waters.

name: Kabir
content:
Tags: state, nation

The northeastern Kingdom of Fire, facing the Sea of Merchants. The wealthiest of the four kingdoms, built on trade rather than tradition. Where Yaif preserves the past and Gissemari creates beauty, Kabir makes money—and uses that money to maintain Neferati influence in a world that would rather forget them.

name: Kadroka
content:
Tags: state, nation, region

Kadroka is a savanna region in southwestern Central Aboyinzu, dominated by open grasslands and the drainage basin of the Royko River system. The region takes its name from the state that controls it—a dragon-ruled territory centered on the capital city of Ponoigari.

name: Kafula
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state in Dalizi.

name: Kama Sa'malina
content:
Tags: lake, geography, dangerous, monsters, wycendeula

The lake district of southern Wycendeula—hundreds of bodies of water scattered across roughly three thousand square miles of boggy forest and scrubland, pressing against the northern edge of the Pale Death. From above, it looks like shattered glass. From the ground, it's a labyrinth of cold water, unstable terrain, and things that hunt.

Kama Sa'malina has no permanent settlements. No roads cross it. The few who enter do so because they're lost, desperate, or stupid. The region's reputation is earned: this is where the continent's isolation and the Pale Death's hostility combine to create a predator's paradise.

name: Kameesi Jungle
content:
The Kameesi Jungle dominates the northwestern peninsula of Urok—a dense, ancient rainforest where the canopy closes so thick that twilight persists even at midday. The air hangs heavy with moisture and the copper-sweet smell of rotting vegetation. This is Zelle territory, and outsiders who enter uninvited rarely leave.

The jungle teems with venomous life: serpents that drop from branches, frogs whose skin kills on contact, insects that burrow beneath flesh to lay eggs. But the true danger is the Zelle themselves—crimson-winged pixies who view every stranger as either threat or sacrifice.

name: Kanüli Daha Plains
content:
Tags: plains, geography

TODO

name: Kazül
content:
Tags: state, nation

A desert kingdom on the southern Middle Sea coast, ruled by King Salomor and his legendary summoning ring. Famous for producing the finest glass in Alaria. A rigid caste system keeps the nobility wealthy and the laborers trapped.

South of Glorin, southwest of Zintacas. The interior is the Jahazai Desert—harsh, ancient, and full of buried ruins.

name: Kelder Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Mountains within Deo Esari.

TODO

name: Kerwin
content:
Tags: state, nation

The northeastern territory of the Tarkhon Empire—though "territory" implies more control than Tarkhon actually exercises. Kerwin is a land of rolling farmland and quiet villages, ruled by an immortal halfling king who predates the empire by centuries.

name: Keshwindi
content:
Tags: citystate, settlement

The city-state of the nameless. Keshwindi rises from the Hills of Dolor like a rebuke to the desert that was meant to kill its founders—a sprawl of mud-brick buildings, shaded markets, and terraced gardens fed by deep wells. Its people are halflings: small in stature, fierce in pride, and bound together by the shared disgrace that brought them here.

name: Kilbyurn Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Klevnaf
content:
Tags: state, nation, winter-elf, istori

The western remnant of the once-unified Winter Elf kingdom, Klevnaf claims legitimacy through Prince Taoinor—uncle to the murdered King Istor XXVI. They control Murder Creek and the sacred Whitewood, drawing spiritual authority from their possession of the land where the ancient kings fell. But legitimacy built on cursed ground comes at a cost.

name: Knova
content:
Tags: state, nation

An archipelago in the northern Western Isles, south of the Iron Sea. Famous as the home of the Iron Academy—the greatest combat school in Alaria—and its sister institutions Seatower and Blackwater.

name: Knulak
content:
Tags: forest, lakes

Knulak is a forest-and-lake region in the north-central Frostmarch Peninsula, named for the cluster of interconnected lakes at its center. Bordered by Barlomew Inlet to the east and the vast Plutachranes forest to the west, Knulak occupies a relatively peaceful stretch of the peninsula—far from the conflicts that plague other regions.

name: Kobuk
content:
Tags: mountains, wurmspine, goblins, cogheads, geography

Kobuk is a rugged mountain territory in the central Wurmspine range, bounded by the Hellion Hills to the north and the Hills to Nowhere to the south. It is home to the surviving Coghead goblins—refugees from two genocides who have built something remarkable in their exile.

The mountains here are steep and defensible, riddled with natural caves and old mining tunnels. The Cogheads have expanded these into a vast underground complex, combining their legendary engineering with desperate necessity. Kobuk is fortress, factory, and hidden city all at once.

name: Korgül
content:
Tags: forest

Home to Greater Satyr

name: Kosko
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state on Agorf Island.

TODO

name: Krell Lands
content:
Tags: jungle, region, ve, krell

A colossal expanse of jungle in southeastern Ve, consumed by the Krell over the past three centuries. What was once a patchwork of human kingdoms, elvish enclaves, and free cities is now a chitinous nightmare—millions of insectoid creatures serving their queens beneath the canopy.

name: Kura
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state in Westrim, occupying the western margins of the Dunes of Kunagi and the southeastern foothills of the mountains that separate Westrim from Tarkhon. Kura lies south of the Righteous Peaks, north of Atriik, and west of the Wyrm Teeth hills. The Ley Line of Fire bisects the nation from north to south, and the entire character of Kura—its government, its magic, its survival—is shaped by this elemental presence.

name: L'Coth D'hari
content: The northern peninsula of the Elder Wilds—a multi-lobed landmass of dense jungle surrounded by the South Sea. L'Coth D'hari is the territory of the **Gamori**, an elven people obsessed with death, transformation, and the sacred hunt.

name: Lake Tonactlet Chipe
content:
The defining geographic feature of the region—a massive freshwater lake roughly 80 miles long and 40 miles wide at its broadest point. The lake serves as the boundary marker between the Dalizi Confederation (to its west/northwest/southwest) and the Dalizi Highlands (to its north/northeast).

Lake Tonactlet Chipe is the economic heart of the Confederation. Its waters teem with fish; its shores produce freshwater pearls; its depths contain mineral deposits that divers harvest at considerable risk. The four Elector states that border the lake—Mpaso, Chilala, Misanda Mulu, and Samani—derive enormous wealth from controlling lakeside ports.

The lake is also sacred to the Lake's Children faith, whose adherents worship it as a living deity. Priests interpret the lake's moods—calm, stormy, murky, clear—as divine messages. Major decisions in lakeshore states are often delayed until the lake "speaks."

Navigation on the lake is treacherous. Sudden storms rise without warning, and the lake floor drops precipitously near certain shores. Skilled pilots command premium fees, and lakeside cities maintain lighthouse networks to guide vessels.

name: Lanwadanzi
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Lenora
content:
Tags: state, nation, elven, confederation

The agricultural heartland of the elven confederation, occupying the fertile lowlands and coastal forests of the western Farlands. Largest of the three kingdoms by population (~400,000), governed by the Landsmeet—a council of landholders.

For full political and cultural details, see the [Lenora state entry](../../nations_and_powers/states/Lenora.md).

name: Lethos
content:
Tags: state, nation, jungle, shacklands

A waterlogged jungle peninsula extending south into the Sea of Sighs, Lethos is one of the smallest and strangest states in the Shacklands. Its five thousand inhabitants—Faeja, Darklings, druids, and the changed folk of Qlyp Augo—live scattered across seven villages in a land that is as much water as earth. The jungle here grows from standing pools; the trees rise from brackish floods; the paths between villages are more canoe routes than roads.

Travelers from Gorath speak of Lethos with a particular silence. The empire's archives note it as "tribute-exempt, do not garrison," and no Gorathi soldier has set foot past the border in three hundred years. The official explanation is that Lethos has nothing worth taking—no farmland, no ore, no population worth assimilating. The unofficial explanation is older, stranger, and spoken only in certain taverns after too much wine: *the jungle remembers*.

name: Lightning Strait
content: TODO

name: Luendrokrül
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A four-hundred-mile expanse of dense forest stretching across Central Aboyinzu, bounded by the Cowardly Mountains to the northwest and thinning into savanna grasslands to the south and east. Unlike its northern neighbor M'Svyla—cultivated, inhabited, deliberately shaped by the Ix'Lorett—Luendrokrül is abandoned. Something lived here once. The forest swallowed it.

name: Luquihn Desert
content:
Tags: desert, geography

The eastern portion of the Sandreach, extending to the Salt Flats at its northeastern tip. Beneath the desert lies a civilization of nomadic merfolk, known as the Murazi.

name: Lyagnadarr Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A rugged mountain range on the eastern edge of the Sandreach Mountains system, forming the northern and western boundary of Siquestrya. The peaks here are lower than the Drystone Mountains to the west but no less treacherous—the slopes are riddled with caves, unstable scree fields, and sudden drop-offs concealed by scrub vegetation.

The Lyagnadarr Mountains are home to the Lyndwyrmspren—lesser draconic creatures that hunt in packs across the mountain slopes. These creatures make the range dangerous for casual travelers but are not the primary threat.

name: Lyoki Ingi Ingi
content:
Tags: jungle, geography, mudzwiti, shacklands

A dense rainforest peninsula stretching south from the Shacklands interior, where the Mudzwiti goblins have built a civilization around death, dinosaurs, and the slow accumulation of glory. The jungle canopy blocks most sunlight, creating a perpetual green twilight beneath where utahraptors hunt in packs and goblin tribes compete for territory, hunting grounds, and the honor of the grandest tombs.

Lyoki Ingi Ingi runs approximately 150 miles from its northern roots to the southern tip, averaging 40-50 miles wide. The terrain is unforgiving—dense undergrowth, sudden ravines, rivers that flood without warning, and megafauna that views humanoids as convenient protein. Outsiders who enter without guides rarely return. The Mudzwiti do not offer guides.

Eight major villages dot the peninsula, each ruled by a tribal king whose lifespan stretches across centuries thanks to rare jungle plants that the Mudzwiti guard jealously. These kings accumulate wealth, followers, and elaborate tomb preparations over generations, competing not for territory but for legacy—who will be remembered longest, whose tomb will stand grandest, whose name will echo through the ages.

name: M'Svyla
content:
Tags: state, nation, forest

A two-hundred-mile stretch of temperate forest running east from the Crystal Mountains into the Central Aboyinzu grasslands. Home to the Ix'Lorett—a lizardfolk people who have turned their homeland into something between a nation and a living gallery.

name: Malo Kon Greikh
content:
Tags: state, nation, giants

The dominant giant nation in the southern Giant Lands, comprising a loose confederation of city-states along the Xcraya River Valley and surrounding highlands. Unlike the savage hill giants of Triuci, the giants of Malo Kon Greikh have built a civilization—crude by human standards, but sophisticated enough to maintain cities, conduct trade, and engage in politics beyond simple violence.

name: Meadow Sound
content:
Tags: sound, geography

A sound in Ve.

TODO

name: Melrin Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

Not mountains at all, but composed of the collosal vertabrae of a titanwurm.

name: Mendari Desert
content:
Tags: desert, geography

The western portion of the Sandreach, separated from the Luquihn Desert to the east by Belu Jenari and the surrounding volcanic badlands. The Mendari is generally considered the more hostile of the two great Sandreach deserts—less water, fewer landmarks, and longer stretches of featureless dunes.

name: Mesyula Bay
content: The western coastline indents around Mesyula Bay, a shallow body of water rich in fish and seabirds. The Korel occasionally fish these waters from small hide boats, but they build no permanent fishing villages. Foreign ships sometimes anchor in the bay to take on fresh water, though doing so risks attracting Korel attention.

name: Micah Inlet
content:
Tags: bay, geography

An inlet in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Misanda Mulu
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Misty Marsh
content:
A vast expanse of shallow water, reed beds, and floating vegetation in western Eoga. The marsh is perpetually shrouded in fog—warm water meeting cooler air creates mist that rarely lifts completely, even at midday. Visibility is often less than 100 feet.

The Misty Marsh is not impassable, but travel requires specialized knowledge. A network of channels connects scattered dry hummocks where small communities survive on fishing, trapping, and harvesting marsh plants. These marsh-dwellers navigate by sound, smell, and an intimate knowledge of the waterways impossible for outsiders to replicate.

The marsh is home to dangerous fauna—giant leeches, poisonous frogs, water serpents, and worse things that hunt in the fog. It also harbors fugitives from all the jungle nations, who disappear into its channels and are never seen again.

name: Misty Marshes
content:
The heartland of Phirexes, a vast expanse of fog-shrouded wetland stretching from the Croaking Briars in the west to the Crocodile Coast in the east. The marshes are not uniform—some areas are relatively solid ground covered in pale grasses, others are treacherous bogs that can swallow a person in minutes. The Dengar have mapped safe paths through generations of trial and fatal error, but the fogs make navigation treacherous even for natives.

The wildlife here has adapted to the perpetual mist. Insects grow large and slow, their compound eyes useless in the murk. Fish have developed lateral lines so sensitive they can navigate by pressure changes alone. The birds have mostly fled or died; those that remain are silent, having learned that calls attract predators they cannot see coming.

name: Misty Valley
content: The **Misty Valley** occupies a low-lying basin at the northeastern corner of the Dalizi Highlands, where the Foggy Mountains give way to the sea. Fed by the Strangle River and perpetually shrouded in fog that spills down from the heights, the valley is the only place along the Shipwreck Coast where the terrain permits anything resembling level ground—though no one has been fool enough to settle it.

name: Moigos
content:
Tags: state, nation

The northern state on Petrona Septia Island, Moigos occupies the lowland forests and river valleys north of the Screech Peaks. The terrain is wet — streams, bogs, and flooded forests dominate the landscape — and the Kappa who rule here prefer it that way. The toad-folk crime lords have built their power in these swampy woodlands, running a state where official government is a polite fiction and real authority flows through debts, favors, and fear.

Moigos settlements are inland, clustered along the two major rivers that drain the region. There's no significant coast — the shoreline is either the supernatural darkness around the Isle of the Night or the treacherous waters of Scatter Sound. Trade that reaches Moigos comes overland or through carefully negotiated passages.

name: Moon Dog
content: TODO

name: Morelous
content:
Tags: plains, ice, geography

The western expanse of Venalthier's frozen interior, stretching from the Crystal Coast in the northwest to the West Selengreyb lowlands in the south. Morelous is marginally more hospitable than Pale Death to the east—not because conditions are better, but because occasional rocky outcrops provide minimal shelter and navigation points.

name: Mountains of Miphoris
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Mountains Semaphois
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Mournful Wood
content:
The largest forest in Chaal Nazzerox, sprawling across the north-central region between Deadman's Lake and the Grave Hills. The Mournful Wood earned its name through a phenomenon no scholar has adequately explained: those who sleep within its boundaries weep uncontrollably through the night. By morning, the tears have crystallized on their faces, sealing their eyes shut.

Victims emerge with tear-tracks of pale crystal running from their eyes to their chins—permanent if not carefully removed. Some have gone blind. Others have been found wandering the forest's edge with their entire faces encased, suffocated by their own sorrow.

The undead pass through the Mournful Wood without difficulty. They don't sleep. They don't weep. Whatever curse afflicts this place targets only the living, which makes it an effective barrier between the northern territories and the south. Refugees fleeing the capital must go around—adding days to their journey and increasing the chance of capture.

name: Movasi
content:
Tags: island, state, nation, geography, pilgrimage, psychedelic

A large island in the southeastern Greenwater Isles, Movasi is covered almost entirely by the **Dream Jungle**—a bioluminescent forest where the boundary between waking perception and something deeper grows dangerously thin. The island is home to the **Velwey**, a reclusive society of dwarves who've built their civilization around the jungle's psychedelic properties and the sacred Pool of Names at their capital.

Pilgrims travel from across the known world to undergo the Velwey ritual: a guided psychedelic journey that can reveal one's **true name**—the magical identity that persists into the afterlife. For those who fear the permanent death that comes when spirits are forgotten in Celestia, Movasi offers something priceless: the chance to learn what name their followers must speak to keep them alive beyond death.

name: Mpaso
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Mueras
content:
Tags: state, nation, coastal

The westernmost state of Rimihuica, Mueras occupies a stretch of brutally fragmented coastline where the land seems to have shattered against the sea. Cliff-walled islands, treacherous channels, and hidden harbors define this region—geography that created the merchant-king oligarchy as surely as any political theory. Those who know the waters control the trade. Those who control the trade rule Mueras.

The coast runs roughly 400 miles north to south, from the Mueras Highlands and Hammer Coast in the north to the Beak of Mueras peninsula in the south. Inland, the arid Dustwind Plateau separates the wealthy coastal cities from the darker eastern reaches—the Taflex Forest, Grimreach, and the shadow-haunted Darkened Coast. The Agreben Sea lies to the south and west; North Agreben island marks the southern extent of Mueran influence.

What outsiders remember about Mueras: the cities carved into living cliff-face, the notorious difficulty of navigation, the wealth that flows through despite everything, and the persistent wrongness that clings to certain stretches of coast. King Kasper's death-curse, when the old tyrant fell, didn't merely haunt Kaspion—it cracked something in the region's relationship with death itself.

name: Mumbling Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography, mysterious

The Mumbling Forest dominates the southwestern coast of Tythikerys, a dense woodland that stretches from Bluesong Bay to the southern reaches of the region. It is named for the constant low sound that pervades it—not quite voices, not quite wind, but something in between that never stops.

name: Murth
content:
Tags: state, nation

A dual-mountain state in the Wurmspine range of western Tarkhon, where Rhea scholar-monks and human merchants share an uneasy but enduring partnership. Two cultures, two capitals, one strange nation bound together by underground tunnels and a shared water source.

name: Nashua
content:
Tags: region, marshland, lizardfolk

Two hundred miles of toxic marshland, tangled jungle, and patient death. Nashua is where invading armies go to disappear. The Ix'Vasyla who rule here have refined defensive warfare into something approaching art—a doctrine of poison, patience, and attrition that has held off both the Gorathi legions and the Strømgodden war-hosts for generations. Every stream hides an ambush. Every clearing is a killing ground. Every step deeper into Nashua is a step closer to a death you won't see coming.

The land itself fights for its people. Toxic fumes rise from stagnant pools. Venomous creatures infest every shadow. The water is undrinkable without treatment. Gorathi soldiers call it "the Poison Country" and speak of comrades who vanished without a trace, entire patrols swallowed by the marshes with nothing left but empty boots. The Ix'Vasyla call it home.

name: Nekanzi Jungle
content:
Tags: jungle, urok, neka, fire-leyline

The Nekanzi Jungle covers the northeastern peninsula of Urok—a dense, humid rainforest split down its center by the Firespine, a raw wound in the earth where the Yolus fire leyline breaches the surface. This is Neka territory, home to the dark-skinned, fire-attuned gnomes who have claimed these lands since before human ships first reached the Greenwater Isles.

The jungle itself is typical of Urok's tropical interior: canopy so thick that the forest floor lives in perpetual green twilight, air heavy enough to drink, and wildlife that treats anything smaller than itself as food. What makes Nekanzi distinct is the heat. Not the humid warmth common to all Urok jungles, but a dry, mineral heat that radiates from the ground itself near the Firespine. Plants here have adapted to periodic burning—fire-blooms that only seed after their parent plant ignites, ash-trees with bark like charcoal that regenerates from within, fungi that fruit in the wake of flames.

The Neka have adapted too. Five villages dot the jungle, loosely confederated by kinship and shared tradition. Two sit directly on the Firespine; three do not. This geographic divide shapes everything about Neka society.

name: Nektuna
content:
Tags: state, nation

The imperial heartland of the Tarkhon Empire, controlling both shores of the Needle—the strait's narrowest point and the key to everything.

name: New Chimea
content:
Tags: state, nation

New Chimea occupies the northeastern corner of the Jungles of Godahi, unique among the five jungle nations for its extensive coastline and direct access to ocean trade. The nation spans the Seawatch Mountains, with settlements scattered across both the jungle interior and the coastal lowlands.

name: Nikpato
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Nirvanis Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

The Nirvanis Mountains were once home to an ancient civilization of people who died out hundreds of years ago to overhunting. All the remains are the massive stone statues dotted throughout the land, under which the dead were buried. These statues were believed to protect the mountains from evil spirits.

The mountains, do, in fact have evil spirits haunting them, which the statues protect against. These spirits are known as Kyth, and appear as white, spectral ghosts. They come out at night and will kill those who are not spiritually warded (such as by the statues).

name: North Agreben
content:
Tags: sea, geography

The northern portion of the Agreben Sea.

TODO

name: Nykotheryx Amberylika
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A dream-warped subregion of the Rootspire Wilds, lying east of Dalizi territory. Known for its reality-bending properties and the dangerous Real Forest within.

See [Nykotheryx Amberylika](../rootspire_wilds/Nykotheryx%20Amberylika.md) for full entry, including details on the Real Forest and Dreamlake.

name: Ofrenia - Tornia
content: Tornia is the northernmost state within Ofrenia, one of the three kingdoms controlled by feuding siblings on the strategically vital island at the heart of the Middle Sea.

name: Okesha's Passage
content: The waterway on the northwest side of Erpeus Island, connecting different parts of the Middle Sea.

name: Old Tolaria
content:
Tags: region, geography, state, nation

A region and former state in Westrim.

TODO

name: Pale Death
content:
Tags: plains, ice, geography, transition

The central-eastern expanse of Venalthier's frozen interior—a vast plain of ice and compacted snow stretching from The Face in the north to the Selengreyb Plateau in the south, and from Morelous in the west to where the ice meets the impossible boundary of Eyendra's living forest. Pale Death is named for what it is: featureless white emptiness where those who enter often don't return.

name: Paradise Inlet
content:
Tags: bay, geography

An inlet in L'Coth D'Hari.

TODO

name: Passage of Hacul
content: TODO

name: Pearly Mountains
content:
The Pearly Mountains arc across SW Urok's midsection, older and more weathered than the Tethryns to the north. Millennia of tropical rain have rounded their peaks into soft shapes that gleam pale gray in certain light—hence the name. The range separates the northern forests and river valleys from the southern territories where Xoth holds sway.

These mountains are riddled with caves. Some are natural limestone formations carved by underground rivers. Others show signs of ancient habitation—smooth walls, carved steps, chambers that once held purpose. The deepest caves connect to tunnel systems that run beneath the entire range, linking to whatever lurks below Maun and Wisgarrd.

The Pearly Mountains' western extent reaches almost to the coast, sheltering Phiona Bay from southern storms. Their eastern slopes descend into the Pyrgion Forest and Xoth territory. In between, the Thyogres River has carved a pass that serves as the main route between the northern and southern halves of SW Urok.

The Myestrones—"hundred streams" in an old trade tongue—drain the northern faces, eventually feeding the Freolian River. The Vyandrik and Prelapon rivers flow west toward Phiona Bay, their valleys dotted with villages that have maintained independence by being too poor and too remote for anyone to bother conquering.

name: Pelaria
content:
Tags: region, kendrel, greenwater-isles

The volcanic kingdom. Pelaria occupies the southern reaches of the eastern Agreben Sea, where underwater volcanic vents have created an environment that would kill ordinary Kendrel—and forged a people adapted to thrive in it.

name: Pelmar Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

The Pelmar Mountains form the northeastern spur of the greater Kharvorn range, rising where the Celedrim Plains give way to the frozen approaches of the Nysanna Range. They serve as a transitional zone between the windswept grasslands to the south and the true arctic to the north—still bitterly cold, but not yet the killing ground of the higher peaks.

name: Penor Syalki
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Central Aboyinzu.

TODO

name: Phenol
content: Tags: island, geography

name: Phirexes
content:
Tags: state, nation, swamp, emerald coast

A fog-shrouded swampland at the southeastern edge of the Emerald Coast, Phirexes is a place of creeping dread disguised as salvation. For sixty years, the gorgon queen Phendexelas the Beautiful has ruled over the Dengar halflings from her city of Maurevelious, having "saved" them from a catastrophe she herself created. The perpetual fogs that blanket the marshes don't just obscure vision—they bind those who stay too long, creating a magical dependency that makes escape nearly impossible.

The landscape is a maze of stagnant pools, thorn-choked wetlands, and forests where the trees seem to watch. What was once a dangerous but livable swamp has become something worse: a prison without walls, where the inmates thank their jailer nightly for keeping them safe from the horrors she unleashed.

name: Phyndarr Sound
content:
Tags: sea, geography

The major waterway connecting the northern Western Isles to the Iron Sea and mainland coast—a broad channel of relatively calm water between the Shattered Sea's chaos and the deeper ocean. Every ship traveling north-south through this region passes through Phyndarr Sound.

name: Plains of Chule
content:
Tags: plains, geography

TODO

name: Plains of Oblivion
content:
Northwest of the mountains of Murth (a state in Tarkhon), stretching from the Western Isles coast in the west to the orc state of Hedroscobb in the northeast, lie the Plains of Oblivion. These cursed grasslands cause all who enter to gradually forget everything except their oldest long-term memories and events from the past year. Recent memories, learned skills, and personal identity slip away like water through fingers.

The plains are home to Satyrn Gray—the palace of Queen Eranes, an extremely powerful half-titan who lost her love when the plains were cursed and split by ancient magic. In her grief, she sought to forget, enchanting her entire palace with powerful spells that hide memories. Though she has succeeded in forgetting her love, she still feels a deep, inexplicable sorrow. She knows she is connected to these plains and cannot bring herself to leave, but she no longer remembers why.

Queen Eranes will handsomely reward anyone who can explain to her what has happened—who she was, what she lost, and why she feels such endless mourning. But the palace's enchantments work against this goal: visitors forget their purpose, the queen forgets the conversations she has, and the truth remains perpetually out of reach. The memory-draining effect of the plains ceases once travelers leave, but the lost memories never return, and the queen remains trapped in her palace of forgotten sorrows.

name: Potalaptuk
content: TODO

name: Poum Titch
content:
Tags: state, nation

A Scalawag goblin nation in southeastern Terrenia, wedged between the Green Mountains to the north, Aal Salma to the west, and the coastal waters of Whitefin Bay to the east. For generations, Poum Titch was known for its raucous festivals, its philosopher-poets, and its peculiar dual-capital system—a compromise that kept the peace between the coastal and inland Scalawag factions.

That peace is over.

name: Predatak
content:
A crater lake in the Celedrim Plains.

TODO

name: Pyceuri Tuni
content:
A tundra region in the Frostmarch Peninsula.

TODO

name: Qet Yemani
content:
Tags: state, nation

Qet Yemani is the largest of the five jungle nations, controlling the western reaches of the Jungles of Godahi. Its vast territory encompasses the great Tora Mazîl forest, extensive swamplands, and the western highlands where the jungle meets the mountains. Where other jungle nations measure their domains in tens of miles, Qet Yemani spans over 120 miles from its eastern borders to the Hekat Valley in the west.

name: Qintas
content:
Tags: state, nation

A coastal state in Central Aboyinzu, home to a significant Amverela high elf population and famous across Alaria for Sondoros wine. Where neighboring Grustos is rugged and complicated, Qintas is refined and deliberate—a place where decisions are made slowly, buildings are built to last centuries, and outsiders are welcomed with patience that can feel like condescension.

The Amverela have lived here for millennia, long enough to have weathered multiple civilizational collapses and rebuilt each time. Their cities were old when human kingdoms were young. Their libraries contain records of eras most scholars consider mythological. They are not immortal, but they live long enough that "long-term planning" means something different to them than to other peoples.

The Light leyline passes through Qintas, continuing from Grustos to the east. The high elves acknowledge its presence but do not worship it as the Kryaaji do—to them, it is simply another feature of the landscape, no more sacred than a river or a hill.

---

name: Rakite
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state in the Farlands.

TODO

name: Raric Bay
content:
Tags: bay, water, geography

A large crescent bay along the southern Innerrim coast where the fish are already dead when you catch them.

name: Red Desert
content:
Tags: desert, geography, ve, sestros

The arid northern reaches of Sestros, where iron-rich sands glow crimson at sunset and nothing grows for months at a time. The Red Desert is technically part of the kingdom, but no one lives there permanently. It's too hot, too dry, too empty.

name: Roule
content:
Tags: island, state, nation, geography

A broad peninsula and island chain in the southern Greenwater Isles, Roule is the heartland of halfling colonial power. Rolling golden plains give way to prosperous farmland, bustling port cities, and—at the center of it all—a dark forest the halflings have never managed to tame. The air smells of honey and salt. Red-roofed towns dot the countryside connected by well-maintained roads, and halfling merchants speak of Roule as civilization's triumph over wilderness. They don't speak of who lived here before.

The halflings arrived three centuries ago. Within two generations, a plague had swept through the indigenous human population, killing nine in ten. The survivors fled into the interior forest. The halflings called it providence and built their empire on the emptied land. Today, Roule is wealthy, orderly, and profoundly unwilling to discuss its founding.

name: Roule Channel
content:
The waterway south of Roule proper, connecting the Bay of Roule to open ocean. The Channel is where light and force leylines intersect beneath the waves—the phenomenon that enables Sunbeam Prison and creates unusual magical conditions for any vessel passing through.

Ships report compasses spinning, minor enchantments flickering, and an oppressive sense of being watched. The Roulean navy has learned to navigate these effects, but foreign vessels sometimes struggle. Whether this is natural leyline interference or deliberate magical manipulation is unclear.

name: Runeglades
content:
The coastal lowlands southeast of Seyiy, where the Elder Wilds meet the Slumbering Abyss.

The Runeglades flood every year. When the seasonal rains come and the rivers swell, the entire region becomes a shallow inland sea. The jungle here has adapted—trees with stilt roots, floating vegetation mats, wildlife that migrates vertically rather than horizontally.

And the dolphins come.

Swimming up from the ocean, pods of freshwater dolphins navigate the flooded forest, hunting fish driven from the deeper channels. They're more intelligent than they should be—following boats, responding to calls, apparently communicating among themselves with clicks and whistles that carry complex information.

The local name "Runeglades" comes from markings found on certain trees and stones—patterns that might be Shemazari writing, might be natural formations, might be something else entirely. During flood season, some of these marked surfaces glow faintly beneath the water. No one has explained why.

---

name: Ryunimir
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in the Westwilds.

name: Samani
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Satyr Lands
content:
Tags: region, geography

A region inhabited by satyrs in Central Aboyinzu.

TODO

name: Scarlet Peaks
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A dramatic mountain range in the southeastern Sandreach Mountains, known for the distinctive red-orange coloration of its rock faces. The color comes from iron-rich stone that oxidizes in the dry mountain air, giving the peaks a bloody appearance at sunset that inspired their name.

name: Scheyolonae
content: TODO

name: Sea of Merchants
content:
Tags: sea, geography

TODO

name: Sea of Sharks
content:
Tags: sea, geography

A cursed expanse of water in the southern Western Isles, infested with sharks so numerous, so aggressive, and so large that they make normal navigation nearly impossible. Ships avoid these waters entirely—unless they're desperate, foolish, or bound for Teku.

name: Sea of Spiders
content: Tags: sea, geography

name: Selengreyb Plateau
content:
Tags: plateau, ice, geography

The elevated heart of Venalthier—a vast icy plateau rising 5,000-6,000 feet above sea level, forming the source region for the glacial flows that define the continent's frozen character. Selengreyb is where the cold is born; the winds that scour Pale Death and Morelous originate here, flowing downhill from this frozen highland.

name: Senaveer Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

The Senaveer Mountains rise northwest of Anu'Itiklik, a rugged barrier between the frozen wastes and the polar approaches. They are home to a large population of northern rocs—massive predatory birds that hunt the caribou herds in the eastern foothills. The mountains are rich with the lichen that feeds the caribou, so the Atowatowa herders journey there every spring, timing their arrival to avoid the rocs' nesting season.

The Senaveer range is notable for its unusual geology: veins of a dark crystalline mineral run through the rock, glittering faintly even in dim light. The Atowatowa consider these veins sacred and refuse to mine them. They call the substance *itikwas*—"frozen tears"—and believe it to be the condensed grief of spirits who could not complete their crossing into Celestia.

name: Septia
content:
Tags: island, geography

An island off the Seacleft Coast.

TODO

name: Sequestria (Siquestrya)
content:
South of the Sandreach Desert and east of the Sandreach Mountains lies Sequestria—a plain savannah wasteland south and west of the Free Isles. North and west of this region rise the Lyagnadarr Mountains, home to Svorinthak the dragon.

Sequestria is home to warring centaur tribes engaged in constant posturing and attempts to assert dominance over each other. Each tribe is ruled by a chieftain who can be challenged by anyone willing to fight for the position. Combat follows mercy rules, and armor is rare—hard to make, slows you down, and inaccessible to most. Most warriors wield spears, bows, and scimitars.

The centaur tribes generally capture and sometimes kill visitors. They don't want anyone interfering with their way of life or their tribal conflicts.

name: Seyiy
content: The central highlands of the Elder Wilds—a dramatic region of hills, waterfalls, and treacherous terrain. Seyiy rises from the surrounding lowland jungle like a rumpled blanket, its rivers carving deep valleys and plunging over cliff faces.

name: Shardspires
content:
Mountain range in the Celedrim Plains.

TODO

name: Shargon
content:
The southern reaches of Konai Mora, where the frozen basin begins climbing toward the mountain ranges that ring the region. Shargon is marginally more hospitable than the central expanse—rocky outcrops provide some shelter from the wind, and the Mythandrulve rarely hunt this far from their preferred terrain.

Small bands of Neth make seasonal camps in Shargon, fishing the ice-locked lakes and hunting the hardier game that survives on the fringes. These camps are temporary—the Neth know better than to claim permanent territory in Konai Mora—but they've used the same sites for generations, leaving behind stone circles and cairns that mark safe camping spots.

name: Shazuihn
content:
Tags: state, nation, orc

The easternmost orc state, occupying the borderlands where the Wildwood thins into the Steppe of Aziirn. The Shazuihni are boar-riding raiders who attack constantly and build nothing permanent. They're the most feared and least understood of the six orc nations.

name: Sheîr
content:
Tags: state, nation

A sprawling island kingdom on the eastern edge of the Western Isles, where the Shattered Sea meets the Tarkhon coast. Home to the Bledreon Islanders—sailors with dual-lid eyes who've worked these waters for centuries.

name: Shemu
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Shepherds' Stones
content:
Tags: mountains, geography, ve, edari, sestros

A dramatic mountain range running east-west across northern Ve, forming the natural border between Edari's empty savannah and the settled lands of Sestros. The range takes its name from the Shailin shepherds who graze their flocks on the southern slopes—the northern side belongs to the Korel.

name: Shunde
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Shyaliviin
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills just south of the Eronia range that poke into the northern edge of the Dunes of Kunagi. This region, also known as Malasi Karkus, is home to the Blitzling—creatures that inhabit these transitional hills between the mountains and the desert sands.

See also: [Dunes of Kunagi](Dunes%20of%20Kunagi.md#shyalaviin-malasi-karkus)

name: Shykasas
content:
Tags: state, nation, shacklands, alekroin

A saltwater swamp kingdom sprawling across a broken archipelago southwest of Kyagos, where the Alekroin have ruled as apex predators for millennia. The landscape is a maze of tidal channels, mangrove thickets, and flooded grasslands—terrain that would swallow armies but yields its secrets to those who evolved within it. Outsiders call it inhospitable. The Alekroin call it home.

Shykasas occupies roughly 400 miles of coastline and island chains between the Sea of Sighs to the north and the open waters to the south. The climate runs hot and humid year-round, with monsoon seasons that raise water levels by several feet and transform the interior into a vast shallow lake. During dry months, the waters recede to expose mudflats teeming with shellfish, waterfowl, and the unfortunate.

The Alekroin do not participate in the Shacklands slave trade. They have no interest in it. Their isolation is not political neutrality—it is the simple indifference of apex predators toward the squabbling of prey. Gorath's slavers give Shykasas waters a wide berth. The few who didn't learned why.

name: Silver Grove
content: The largest Albersea island, named for the metallic-leafed trees that grow nowhere else. The leaves aren't actually silver, but they ring like bells when the wind blows. A small monastery maintains the grove, accepting pilgrims who wish to hear the "singing forest."

name: Singa Chike
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Slaver's Coast
content:
Tags: coast, geography, shacklands

See [Pesalolo](Pesalolo.md#slavers-coast) for full details.

The southern coast of Pesalolo, where Gorath operates its slave extraction operation. Ships run port-to-port collecting Drasnian dwarves for transport through Divinity Passage to Azantir.

name: Smokey Bay
content: TODO

name: Soki
content: TODO

name: Sokorgül

Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Central Aboyinzu.

name: Soros
content:
Tags: island, geography

An island in the Western Isles.
