name: South Suki
content:
Tags: jungle, geography

A jungle within Chimea.

TODO

name: Soya Grei
content: TODO

name: Stipen
content:
Tags: state, nation

An Ezuri (Tree Gnome) gerontocracy in the forests along the Chedwin Strait. The entire country is ancient woodland where gnomes live in tree-settlements and can survive for 500 years—so long as they never leave.

East of Thespia, north of Zintacas, with coastline along the Chedwin Strait.

name: Stonedrift
content:
A navigational hazard and curiosity: a field of massive floating stones that drift through the central Alrock on unpredictable paths. The stones are porous volcanic rock, somehow light enough to float. Some are large enough to support vegetation. One supports a hermit's shack.

Ships navigate around Stonedrift carefully. The stones move slowly but unpredictably, and collisions are common. Some captains swear the stones deliberately block certain routes—as if they're herding ships toward something.

name: Strait of Curses
content:
The waters south of Xabraedia and north of Hagswood are known as the Strait of Curses, named for the hags who prowl these treacherous waters. The strait is also inhabited by large, nasty serpents known as Silkwurms—creatures that can wrap entire ships in silk and knot them to the seafloor, dragging vessels and crews to their doom. The hags of Hagswood maintain an uneasy alliance with these serpents, communicating with them and occasionally working together to claim victims.

Ships that must cross the Strait of Curses do so quickly, in convoy if possible, and never at night. Even then, losses are common. The hags seem to know which ships carry valuable cargo—or valuable passengers.

name: Stratlan Sea
content:
Tags: sea, geography

The deep, empty waters west of the Western Isles—the edge of the known world. Beyond the Stratlan Sea lies nothing that any sailor has found and returned to report. Ships that venture too far west don't come back.

name: Sunrise Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Mountains within Shyona.

TODO

name: Sunshatter Palace
content:
A structure of impossible geometry hovering above the waves, home to a being called the Seahorse King—supposedly a god from another plane of existence. The Palace is the only place this entity can survive in Alaria; something about the intersection of sea, sky, and otherworldly geometry keeps it anchored to reality.

The Seahorse King grants audiences to those who reach the Palace, offering cryptic prophecies and occasionally material aid. What it wants in return is unclear. Visitors report that the interior of the Palace exists in more dimensions than the exterior suggests.

name: Sunshine Coast
content: The northeastern coastal stretch where the Luquihn Desert meets the sea. Named for the relentless, unbroken sunlight that bakes this shore—there is little cloud cover this far into the desert's rain shadow. The coast is rocky and inhospitable, with few natural harbors. Small communities of salt-harvesters and fishermen eke out an existence here, trading with passing ships from the Free Isles.

name: Swampdeep
content: A saltwater swamp and bayou region that technically separates Ofrenia from the Rimihuican mainland. Located south of Ofrenia, appearing as a cloudy white band between the Gray Sea and the Oblivion storm to the east.

name: Syhkasas
content: Tags: state, nation

name: Sylke
content: A state on Ofrenia.

name: Tangiern
content:
Tags: state, nation, human

The largest state on the Crimson Coast by territory, Tangiern is a human kingdom that has carved civilization from the vast Fragenstor Mielthøn forest over centuries. While their elven neighbors tear themselves apart in civil war, the Tangier people watch and wait—knowing their survival depends on choosing the right moment to act.

name: Tanglos Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

TODO

name: Tarkhetan
content:
Tags: city, capital

The capital city of the Tarkhon Empire.

TODO

name: Tarn
content:
Tags: state, nation, orc

An orc state in the north-central Wildwood, controlling a strategic stretch of the Grand Tolkarsus river where it passes through the Plenjorn Swamp. The Tarni are spirit warriors—orcs whose souls are so firmly anchored to their bodies that death struggles to claim them.

name: Taulo Magi
content: A state in Dalizi.

name: Telwood
content:
Tags: forest, geography

The southernmost of the three eastern forests, Telwood lies between Milrar Forest to the north and the Grieb Lakes region to the southwest. Unlike its mysterious northern neighbor, Telwood is thoroughly mapped, frequently traveled, and historically significant—it was the site of the first diplomatic contact between the expanding orc states and the human settlements of the northern Westwilds.

name: Tenekee Woods
content:
Tags: forest, geography

The transitional forest between the Krenglelan foothills and the Walking Forest proper, the Tenekee Woods are what hunters call "honest woods"—dangerous, yes, but predictable. The rules here are the rules.

name: The Apelands
content:
South of L'Coth D'hari, across Farthing Inlet, lies a stretch of rainforest dominated by its most dangerous inhabitants: the great apes.

These are not ordinary primates. The apes of the Apelands stand forty feet tall, covered in dark fur, possessed of terrifying strength and violent territorial instincts. They roam the forest in loose bands, battling each other for dominance, and killing anything they perceive as a threat.

No one has successfully studied them. Expeditions that enter the Apelands either return within days or don't return at all. What little is known comes from observation at the boundaries:
- They are omnivorous but prefer meat
- They communicate through deep vocalizations that carry for miles
- They appear to have some form of social hierarchy, though the specifics are unclear
- They occasionally venture south into Seyiy and Bloodwood, causing devastation

Why apes of this size evolved here—whether natural adaptation or magical intervention—remains unknown. Some scholars note that the Anchor Trees are particularly numerous in and around the Apelands.

---

name: The Aphenlein Forests
content: West of Seyiy, descending toward Thrumbling Bay, the terrain flattens dramatically. Here the Elder Wilds transform into something different: the Aphenlein forests, where the jungle becomes as much water as land.

name: The Ashy Mountains
content:
The Ashy Mountains are a range of active volcanoes running across central Aknimchuk. Unlike the fire-leyline heat of Blizrynthine far to the east, these are natural volcanoes—mountains of stone and fire that have been erupting for millennia. The peaks constantly emit smoke and ash, giving the range its name and leaving a grey pallor over the southern half of the island.

The volcanic activity creates a microclimate where life can survive. Hardy shrubs and mosses grow on the lower slopes, and hot springs dot the valleys between peaks. Strange creatures make their homes here—things that have adapted to the heat and cannot survive in the frozen wastes beyond.

name: The Basin
content: The great basin that forms Anarak's territory—grassland and farmland stretching between the mountains. Good agricultural land, heavily taxed, worked by a population that believes their sacrifices serve a noble cause.

name: The Deadloop (Inner Sea)
content:
A large inland sea cutting into the continental interior. Key features around the Deadloop:
- **Echem Yiakraxes**: Large plains in the northwestern portion of the Deadloop
- **Donclik Sound**: Body of water due east of Echem Yiakraxes
- **Mikisapi Forest**: An island/peninsula within the Deadloop, encircled by the waterway
- **Tenches**: A city-state on Mikisapi Forest

name: The Forest
content:
M'Svyla is old growth, the kind of forest that remembers when different creatures walked beneath its canopy. Broadleaf trees dominate—oak, ash, and species with no common tongue names—their crowns forming a broken ceiling that filters light into green-gold shafts. The understory is dense but navigable, threaded with game trails that the Ix'Lorett have walked for generations.

The forest thins as it stretches east, transitioning gradually from dense woodland to scattered copses to the open savanna of interior Aboyinzu. The western edge presses against the Crystal Mountain foothills, where mountain streams feed into the forest's river network. Xia Lake sits in the south-central forest, a broad shallow body of water ringed by some of the oldest Ix'Lorett settlements.

The Esosa River flows down from the north, cutting through the forest's eastern reaches before continuing south into the grasslands. Smaller tributaries web through the woodland, and the Ix'Lorett have built their villages along these waterways for as long as anyone can remember.

name: The Impact Craters
content: Three massive craters punctuate the Dreamplains, remnants of an ancient cataclysm when objects fell from the sky in the distant past. Whether these were meteors, fragments of the Astral Plane, or something else entirely remains debated by scholars.

name: The Kendrel Kingdoms
content:
Three Kendrel states occupy the eastern Agreben, collectively known as the Kendrel Kingdoms despite their distinct governments and occasional rivalry:

**Flurin** (north): The cosmopolitan gateway, handling most trade with surface nations. See [Flurin](Flurin.md).

**Glador** (center): The traditional heartland, largest and most insular of the three. See [Glador](Glador.md).

**Pelaria** (south): The volcanic kingdom, fire-attuned and militarized. See [Pelaria](Pelaria.md).

The kingdoms maintain formal alliance against external threats (primarily Bfaspeen) while competing fiercely in trade, prestige, and the elaborate courtship-politics that define Kendrel society.

name: The Knives
content:
A mountain range in the Kharvorn Mountains region.


The Knives, as they are commonly called, are in a constant state of very high winds, which have blown the peaks into sharp points, giving the mountains their name.

name: The Suki Jungle
content:
The dense tropical jungle that covers much of Chimea's territory, divided into three regions:

**West Suki** — Stretches from the Blood Mountains to the coast. The most settled region, with established roads and harvesting operations. Towns like Miltalie and Kargunyah anchor the interior.

**South Suki** — The southern interior, wilder but productive. The Tonyfall River runs through it, with Aldaville and Frowat controlling river traffic. The capital Chimus sits where the jungle opens into cultivated plains.

**North Suki** — Borders the Boneswamps and Dern. The most dangerous region—close to enemy territory and haunted by the undead. Settlements like Bowning, Kinglake, and Chatford watch the swamps nervously.

name: The Twin Cities
content:
Yuki and its sister city Garlow exist in unusual symbiosis. Founded by refugees from the fallen Jaipon Confederacy roughly two centuries ago, the two cities were originally rival settlements competing for the same hilltop. When the first major Krell probe reached the hills in 3180 SD, the cities realized they would survive together or die separately.

Today, Yuki handles defense and diplomacy; Garlow handles production and supply. The cities are connected by a fortified road and share a common council, though each maintains its own internal governance. In practice, they function as a single entity with two geographic centers.

name: The White Wastes
content:
Tags: desert, geography

A vast white desert in Wycendeula.

Miscellaneous orc and goblin tribes ravage the desert.

Home to Nashk, the Alchemist

name: The Widebarrow Mountains
content:
A moderate-sized mountain range runs diagonally from the southwest to the northeast, roughly bisecting the continent. From south to north, it comprises:

- **Blood Mountains** (south): Home to an underground realm of vampiric gnomes
- **Mez**: Mountain state
- **Dern**: Mountain state
- **Thorum** (north): Northernmost mountain state

name: The Wind-Touched Plains
content: The entire steppe is slightly magical. Wind attunement seeps into the land, the horses, and the people. Products made here carry faint enchantments of speed and air. The Ta Minn cavalry seem to ride faster than physics should allow.

name: Thespia
content:
Tags: state, nation

A human agricultural kingdom in chaos. Recently seized by Queen Akta, a mad mercenary sorceress from the south. The kingdom lives in the shadow of Morgnor, King of the Mountains, whose dragon territory lies to the northwest.

East of Morgnor's mountains, north of Glorin, west of Stipen, southwest of Ta Minn.

name: Thrumbling Bay
content:
The bay on the western coast where the rivers of Aphenlein empty into the South Sea. Thrumbling Bay earned its name from the sound—the constant low rumble of water flowing from dozens of river mouths, creating a background noise that visitors describe as both soothing and unsettling.

The bay itself is shallow, the water brown with silt, the coastline a maze of river deltas and mangrove-like vegetation. This is not a natural harbor—ships cannot easily approach, and there's nothing to approach for. Thrumbling Bay marks the edge of the navigable world. Beyond lies only the endless green of the Aphenlein, and beyond that, the hostile heights of Seyiy.

---

name: Thundering Mountains
content: The **Thundering Mountains** form the great northern wall of the Dalizi Highlands, a massive range that separates the highland interior from the Wanderlands to the north. The mountains earn their name from the constant rumble of avalanches, rockfalls, and—according to some—sounds that have no natural explanation. This is the highest and most formidable range in the Dalizi Highlands, and it has only one known crossing.

name: Tonactlet Chipe
content:
Tags: lake, geography

TODO

name: Tornia
content: A state on Ofrenia.

name: Traevluong
content:
The eastern coastal jungle, running from the Eskagon Hills down to the southern waters. Dense rainforest, heavy with moisture from the Alrock Ocean, where the canopy grows so thick that twilight reigns even at midday.

This is werewolf territory.

name: Trømgar
content:
Tags: region, mountains, dwarves

Stone, steel, and blood. Trømgar rises from the Thunderdark Peaks like a clenched fist—a nation of fortress-cities carved into mountainsides, inhabited by dwarves who have made warfare itself into religion. The Strømgodden do not merely fight; they worship through combat, offering every battle as sacrifice to their war-gods and measuring a life's worth by the glory of its ending.

Every settlement here is a fortress. Every citizen is a warrior. Every piece of armor bristles with spikes designed to turn the wearer's body into a weapon. The Strømgodden have held these mountains against lizardfolk from the south and whatever lurks in the briars to the east for generations, and they will hold them until the mountains themselves crumble—or until they die gloriously trying.

name: Turquish Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography

A bay in Ve.

TODO

name: Turxis Golathi
content:
The northern section of the Twaan Forests, Turxis Golathi is the oldest and densest part of the woodland. Ancient trees tower here, their canopy so thick that the forest floor lies in permanent twilight. This is where the most established Twaan clans make their homes, their burrow-networks extending deep into the earth.

Turxis Golathi is relatively peaceful by Twaan standards—the clans here have sorted out their territorial disputes generations ago, and a rough hierarchy keeps the endless bickering from erupting into open conflict. Outsiders are rare this deep in the forest, and the Twaan prefer it that way.

The Earth ley line runs through the southern edge of Turxis Golathi before curving southeast toward the Magma Pits. The soil here is unnaturally fertile, and the trees grow to enormous size. Some Twaan claim the oldest trees are aware in some fashion, though they won't elaborate to outsiders.

name: Twilight Forest
content:
Tags: forest, spindral, elves, darkness

A vast forest shrouded in supernatural darkness in the southeastern reaches of the Frostmarch Peninsula. The Twilight Forest is one of the most dangerous and forbidding places in all of Clueanda—a realm where light itself has been cursed.

name: Tykos
content:
Tags: state, nation, island, geography

Tykos is an island chain south of Erpeus, home to minotaur clans who've practiced slavery for longer than most civilizations have existed. The islands are rocky, resource-poor, and strategically positioned at a crossroads of Middle Sea trade routes. The minotaurs turned these disadvantages into a brutal economy: what they can't grow, they take; what they can't use, they sell.

The slave markets of Tykos are famous—or infamous—across Alaria. Buyers come from nations that publicly condemn the practice, purchasing trained workers, household servants, and worse. The minotaurs don't pretend to be anything other than what they are. They find the hypocrisy of their critics amusing.

name: Tythikerys
content:
Tags: plains, region, orc territory

Tythikerys is a vast stretch of arid scrubland in southwestern Central Aboyinzu, homeland of the Tytheri blood orcs. The region is largely empty of permanent settlement—the orcs are raiders and harvesters, not builders—but it serves a crucial strategic purpose: it separates Kadroka from the coast, landlocking the dragon-ruled state and denying it maritime trade.

name: Ubrik
content:
Tags: state, nation

A Uline dwarf nation in the mountains of the Middle Sea coast, between Camaran and Bestacia. Once a prosperous mining state, now a nightmare. The silvertongue outbreak has turned half the population into mercury-mouthed cannibals, and the infection is spreading.

Member of the Aldriktch Trade Alliance, though the alliance is buckling under the crisis. Refugees flood neighboring states. Silvertongues raid across borders. No cure exists.

name: Uhionai
content:
Tags: state, nation, orc

The far northwestern orc state, occupying the ancient Ryunimir Forest. The Uihonaii are the tree-bound orcs—they become trees when they die and can be reborn from their wooden forms. Their territory is less a nation and more a living graveyard of ancestors.

name: Ulkevolm
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state in the Luquihn Desert region.

TODO

name: United Free Nations
content:
Tags: state, nation

TODO

name: Untouched Sea
content:
Tags: sea, geography

A sea bordering Central Aboyinzu.

TODO

name: Vogenfeld
content:
Tags: state, nation

The northern shield of Tarkhon—three Uline dwarf settlements in the Wurmspine Mountains between the empire and the orcish hordes of Hedroscobb. For centuries, the dwarves have held the passes. For centuries, they've watched the Wildwood. And for decades now, they've wondered why they bother.

name: Wadiyah
content:
Tags: state, nation

The southernmost and most isolated of the Kingdoms of Fire. While other kingdoms counsel patience, accommodation, or indifference, Wadiyah remembers the Severance with burning anger. Queen Khalira believes the time for waiting has ended. The fire kingdoms should reclaim what is theirs—and if the other monarchs won't act, Wadiyah will.

name: Watar
content:
Tags: state, nation

A Dwarblin merchant state on a peninsula in the western Middle Sea. Northwest of the Telphineas Strait, south of Erasnus, with Echea at the peninsula's tip beyond them. Ruled by an oligarchy of trading families, Watar is scrappy, entrepreneurial, and hungry for more.

Member of the Aldriktch Trade Alliance, though increasingly straining against it. Relations with Bestacia are worsening over control of the Telphineas Strait.

name: Watchkeep Isles
content:
A small archipelago off the western coast of the Beak, comprising three inhabited islands and numerous rocks and shoals. The islands—home to Indus, Freed, and Giorga—were historically watchtowers guarding the southern approaches to Labyrinth Sound. The old stone keeps still stand, though they now serve as administrative centers rather than military posts.

**Indus**: The largest of the Watchkeep Isles, home to perhaps two thousand people. The old watchtower has been converted into a lighthouse, and Indus serves as the pilot station for ships approaching from the south. House Marsova of Hennipin technically claims authority here, but exercises it lightly.

**Freed**: Named for the freed slaves who settled here after Kasper's fall—Kasper had maintained slave galleys, and the rebellion liberated their crews. The descendants maintain a fierce independence and a deep hatred of anything resembling centralized authority. They fish, they trade, they emphatically do not pay taxes to any merchant-king.

**Giorga**: The smallest inhabited island, little more than a fishing village clustered around a natural harbor. Notable mainly for its shrine to the sea-gods, which draws occasional pilgrims.

name: West Selengreyb
content:
The western margin of the plateau descends through a broad transition zone called West Selengreyb. This region—roughly 60 miles wide—represents the slope between the high plateau and the lower Morelous plains.

West Selengreyb is where the katabatic winds are born. Cold, dense air pools on the plateau surface, then flows downhill under its own weight. The slope accelerates this flow, creating persistent winds of 40-60 mph that blast westward toward the coast. During cold snaps, these winds can exceed 100 mph—hurricane force—for days at a time.

The transition zone is worse than either the plateau or the plains. It combines the plateau's extreme cold with the plains' exposure, while the wind accelerates to its maximum velocity precisely in this band. No shelter exists; the slope is too consistent to create wind shadows. Crossing West Selengreyb is the most dangerous leg of any east-west journey through Venalthier.

name: West Suki
content:
Tags: jungle, geography

A jungle within Chimea.

TODO

name: West Whitewall
content:
Tags: cliffs, glacier, geography

The western terminus of the great Whitewall—a continuous ice cliff stretching across southern Venalthier where the continental glaciers meet the sea. West Whitewall marks where the Scepter Mountains disappear beneath the ice sheet, their peaks becoming mere nunataks before vanishing entirely into the frozen mass.

name: Windor
content:
Tags: state, nation, region

Windor occupies the northern coast of Terrenia, a wedge of fertile lowland backed by the forested bulk of the Green Mountains. The land slopes from mountain foothills in the south down to a rocky coastline in the north, cut by rivers draining snowmelt toward the sea. It's a country of orchards and pastures, stone-walled villages, and distant peaks that always seem closer than they are.

The Windorf halflings who call this place home have shaped it into something comfortable—roads well-maintained, inns frequent, hedgerows trimmed. But the mountains loom. Smoke rises from unseen valleys. And every village within a day's walk of the treeline keeps its gates sturdy and its signal fires ready.

name: Winger Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography, ve, krell

A smaller range in the central-southern Krell Lands, the Winger Mountains are less densely populated by the insects than their northern counterpart. Something about these peaks makes the Krell uncomfortable—they patrol the perimeter but rarely establish permanent hives within.

name: Wolfspine
content:
A small mountain range in the Celedrim Plains.

TODO

name: Wurmling Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

Not mountains at all, but composed of the collosal vertabrae of a titanwurm. This was the first of the titanwurm mountains to be discovered.

name: Wurn Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Part of the greater Wurmspine range, the Wurn Mountains form Enymu's northern border. Home to the Stone Men—a human subculture whose gray, rock-hard skin marks generations of elemental exposure. Switzerland vibes: alpine meadows, dramatic peaks, isolated valleys, and a people who want to be left alone.

name: Xabraedia
content:
Tags: island, geography

An island in the Free Isles.

name: Xoth
content:
The theocratic nation of Xoth dominates southeastern Urok—a multi-racial covenant-state bound by shared worship of the Seven Patrons rather than blood or ancestry. See the full state entry for details on its religion, government, and the daemon-aided military that makes its neighbors nervous.

Xoth's territory spans diverse terrain: sacred forests, fertile plains, harsh desert, and a long coastline. The landscape shapes the faith as much as the faith shapes the landscape; every region has its patron, every feature its religious significance.

name: Yaif
content:
Tags: state, nation

The westernmost of the Kingdoms of Fire, and the oldest. Home to the Flame of First Fire, the Temple of Origins, and the ancient traditions that define Neferati identity. If the other kingdoms are the children of fire, Yaif is the mother.

name: Yukum's Wall
content: Although not actually a wall, this mountain range is rife with giants, wyverns, snow spirits, and an eternal blizzard, making passing through so treacherous that it has almost never been done. Located on the east side of Konai Mora (basin plains surrounded by mountains in northeast all the way to southwest). Yukum's Wall is coastal mountains, separating Celedrim Plains from the Northern Isles. To its north are the Blistering Mountains (contiguous with Yukum's Wall, volcanoes).

name: Zelidia
content: The state of Zelidia encompasses all Zelle settlements along the Sovia River. See the full state entry for details on its theocratic government and blood-sacrifice religion.

name: Zemkach
content: TODO

name: Zintacas
content:
Tags: state, nation

A tribal Gozi (Goshwen) nation in the canyon country south of Stipen. Red rock gorges, towering stone spires, mesas, and endless sky. Utah/Arizona vibes—harsh, beautiful, and deadly to outsiders who don't know the terrain.

The Gozi fly on great raptors that hunt the canyons. Tribal society, no central government, deeply isolated, and they like it that way.

name: Zlyverth Korn
content: The central forest is where the fire ley line enters Twaan territory, running north-south along the eastern edge of the woodland before converging with the Earth ley line at the Magma Pits. This convergence has created something extraordinary: the Burning Corridor.

name: Adi Assabehta
content:
The capital city, built into and around the larger of the two Iri peaks. A vertical city of towers, bridges, and carved chambers, home to the greatest universities in Alaria. Paragon-attuned elementalists teach here in every discipline. Students come from across the world to study, and work on magical artifacts part-time to fund their education.

**The Secret:** A mad scholar opened a portal to Zylogmus Eternus, binding the entire city to the flipside of the Material Plane. People are mysteriously dying—slowly, inexplicably. The scholar was the first to die. No one knows about the portal. Most residents don't believe the deaths are connected, attributing them to various causes. Over the coming years, the capital will slowly empty as people flee what they don't understand.

name: Adrak
content: The capital city, seat of the monarchy, and home to the Royal Adrak Bank. Sits on or near the water ley line. The nobility here keep domesticated dragons as pets—impressive creatures, but shadows of the war-beasts their ancestors once rode. The city exudes old wealth and older pride.

name: Aikon Tidal Islands
content:
Tags: archipelago, geography

A chain of low-lying islands north of Sheîr where the tides reshape reality twice daily. At high tide, only scattered peaks rise above the water. At low tide, a vast expanse of land emerges—temporary territory that the Aikon people have learned to use.

name: Al Iwash
content:
Tags: citystate

An independent city-state. The name suggests desert or Neferati cultural influence.

TODO: Determine location, develop government, culture, economy, and notable features.

name: Al Maqlubah
content:
Tags: citystate

An independent city-state. The name suggests desert or Neferati cultural influence.

TODO: Determine location, develop government, culture, economy, and notable features.

name: Alacayir
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Aldrichold
content:
The capital of Enymu, sitting at the junction of the Greenway (the main road to Tarkhetan) and the river that waters the central farmlands.

Aldrichold is more large town than true city—perhaps 15,000 people, mostly involved in administration, trade, and supporting the royal household. The palace is a fortified manor house, comfortable but not grand. The Aldric kings have never seen the point of showing off.

The town has a central market, several temples (including one to the Evertorch, modestly attended), guild halls for the craftsmen, and warehouses where grain is stored before shipment to Nektuna.

name: Ámas Kiori
content: The sacred basin north of Adrak, holy land to all dragonborn. The entire region is considered sacred ground.

name: Anemone Sound
content:
South of Chitinwood, the coast opens into Anemone Sound—named for the vast colonies of sea anemones that carpet its shallow floor. These are not ordinary anemones. The largest specimens stretch twenty feet across, their stinging tentacles capable of paralyzing a whale.

The anemones have made the sound nearly impassable. Ships that enter find their hulls gripped by tentacles, their crews snatched from the decks. The water itself seems to reach upward. Only shallow-draft vessels piloted by those who know the safe channels can navigate through, and that knowledge is jealously guarded.

Something feeds the anemones—something that wants the sound protected. The waters beyond the colonies are strangely clear, almost crystalline, and divers who've made it through report structures on the seafloor: walls, columns, what might be roads. No one has explored them. No one has returned from trying.

name: Aphenlein
content: Tags: forest, geography

name: Arins Plains
content:
The agricultural heart of Roule stretches across the northern and central regions—endless fields of grain, orchards, and most importantly, the flower farms that drive Roulean wealth. Taberthine blossoms (pale blue, faintly luminescent at dusk) produce honey that sharpens the mind, prized by scholars and merchants across Alaria. Zindwatt flowers (deep crimson, almost black at the center) yield a honey that induces gentle euphoria, technically regulated but freely available to those with coin.

The plains are beautiful in a manicured way. Windmills turn constantly—Roule is never without wind—and the halfling estates sprawl white and comfortable amid their holdings. The laborers in the fields are a mix of halfling smallholders, contracted workers, and slaves. Slavery in Roule is "modest," they'll tell you. A small household staff. Domestic service. The Honey Lords don't discuss the field crews.

The wind shapes everything here. Buildings are low and sturdy. Trees grow bent. Halflings greet each other with "fair winds," and the worst curse you can level is to wish someone "still air."

name: Ashen Hills
content:
A range of dry, pale hills running through Aal Salma's interior, named for their ash-colored stone and thin, dusty soil. Little grows here except scrub grass and hardy thornbushes. The hills are hot in summer, cold in winter, and unpleasant year-round.

Despite their barrenness, the Ashen Hills hold value. Salt deposits in the eastern reaches supply Aal Salma's preserving industry. Ancient caves riddle the rock, used variously by hermits, outlaws, and—allegedly—pre-human cultures whose carvings still mark the deepest chambers. Miners and scholars both venture into the hills, though neither finds exactly what they're looking for.

name: Ataç
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Atra
content: The capital, such as it is—the largest tree-settlement in Stipen. Home to the council of elders who govern the forest realm. Built into and around massive ancient trees, with bridges and platforms connecting homes high above the forest floor.

name: Attla
content:
Tags: village, settlement

A village on Phyr Island.

TODO

name: Avakus Forest
content:
A stretch of dense woodland west of Syvlius, climbing into the mountain foothills. Before the revolution, Syvlius used Avakus Forest as a buffer zone—patrols maintained order, logging operations extracted timber, and escaped slaves who fled this direction were hunted down.

Now Avakus is contested ground. Dead Moon warriors pass through heading south. Drasnian refugees hide in its depths. Sylthik survivors from Syvlius shelter in abandoned logging camps. And the Kuru, sensing weakness, have begun probing north from the Killing Hills.

name: Awobiso
content:
Tags: city, state, nation, free-isles

The gatekeepers of the Free Isles, where paranoia is policy and strangers are screened before they're welcomed. Awobiso sits at the western edge of the archipelago, the first port of call for ships arriving from Upoceax and the Scaley Sea. The Marrovini family has controlled this city for generations, building their power on shipyards, smuggling networks, and the simple fact that everyone passes through their waters first.

name: Ayashk Island
content: Tags: island, geography

name: Azantir
content:
The capital. Where the Lucenia River empties into the Divinity Passage, Azantir sprawls across both banks in a maze of pyramids, canals, and markets. This is the largest city in the Shacklands—estimates put the population at over 200,000, though accurate counts are impossible given the Skaag warrens below and the constant flow of slaves through the markets.

The Emperor's Palace crowns the city's highest pyramid, offering views across the entire urban expanse. Below it, the Imperial Plaza can hold fifty thousand for speeches, festivals, or public executions. The great Pillar of Conquest here stands over two hundred feet tall, covered in names too numerous to read.

Azantir's docks never sleep. Slave ships arrive from Tamadrez. Timber rafts float down from upriver. Trade goods flow in from across Alaria, drawn by Gorath's colossal economy. If it can be bought or sold, Azantir has a market for it.

name: Azkirk
content:
In the western Eceraen foothills, where the mountains meet the coast, stands a lone marker that appears on some maps as Azkirk. The name is old—possibly pre-satyr—and its meaning is lost.

What Azkirk marks is unclear. Some maps show it as a settlement, others as a point of interest, still others as a warning. The location corresponds to a rocky headland where the mountain slopes plunge directly into the sea, creating a natural landmark visible from ships offshore.

Those who've investigated report finding nothing but stone—no structures, no artifacts, no obvious significance. Yet the name persists across centuries of cartography, copied from map to map by scribes who know only that something important once happened there, or will happen, or is happening still.

Local satyr avoid the area, claiming the ground is "marked." They don't explain what this means. The ulvsjael don't come this far south regardless. Whatever Azkirk represents, it remains a minor mystery in a region full of greater ones.

name: Azuros
content:
Tags: state, nation

Azuros clings to the highest peaks of northern Erpeus, a collection of stone villages surrounding the ancient Shogi Monastery. The wind here never stops—it scours the rocks, howls through the passes, and shapes everything about life on the mountain. The monks who live here have learned to work with that wind, developing air magic techniques that produce results other practitioners consider impossible.

Reaching Azuros requires climbing through Lier's Murder Hills and then ascending paths that would challenge mountain goats. Most people never try. Those who do arrive in a place that feels untouched by time—the same stone buildings, the same rituals, the same silence that has defined the monastery for centuries.

name: Baerlüen Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Banesnap Hills
content: The **Banesnap Hills** run along the eastern face of the Foggy Mountains, forming a broken barrier of ridges and valleys between the mountain wall and the Shipwreck Coast. The hills take their name from the banesnap beetle, an insect whose bite causes agonizing muscle cramps—the hills are home to the largest known population of these creatures in Alaria.

name: Bani Bari
content:
The capital of Kura sits at the heart of the nation, carefully positioned to straddle the Ley Line of Fire. The city is divided into two halves—the Sunward District to the east of the ley line, where fire-attuned Yngli predominate, and the Shadeward District to the west, home to most of the water-attuned Nuun.

The Twin Throne sits at the exact center, a palace of fused glass and sandstone built directly over the ley line. Here the blue-eyed Nuun king and the golden-born Yngli ruler hold court together, their combined attunements moderating the intense magical energies beneath them. The palace architecture channels ley line heat into the city's famous glass foundries while the water crown ensures drinking water flows in the central fountain.

**Population**: ~45,000

The city is famous for its glass—windows, vessels, lenses, and art pieces of extraordinary clarity and color. The foundries run day and night, drawing on the ley line's power. Glass caravans depart weekly for the coastal ports and the dwarven cities to the northwest.

name: Bata Nujo Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in Gorath.

TODO

name: Bay of Lords
content: A bay on Urok.

name: Bay of Roule
content:
The great southern bay that gives Roule its naval power and commercial reach. Sheltered from the worst storms by the eastern peninsula, the Bay of Roule offers deep anchorage and calm waters. Teldib dominates the bay's northern shore, but fishing villages and minor ports scatter along the coastline.

The bay is heavily patrolled. Roulean naval vessels run regular circuits, and privateering is punished by imprisonment or worse. The message is clear: these waters belong to Roule, and Roule protects its interests.

name: Belanora
content:
Tags: region, hills, geography, ve

A rugged hill country along the western edge of Meadow Sound, separating the coastal waters from the interior jungles. The Belanora hills rise abruptly from the lowlands, their eastern faces forming dramatic cliffs that overlook Belanorn Bay and the sound beyond.

name: Belanorn Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography, ve, meadow sound

A sheltered bay at the western end of Meadow Sound, where the Misery River deposits its waters into the sea. The bay serves as the primary maritime access point for the southern jungles of Ve, though the river's influence makes it an uneasy place.

name: Benshu's Promise
content: This oasis is located in the Dunes of Kunagi, and is the only source of freshwater for hundreds of miles. It is guarded by Benshu, a large, incredibly deadly sphinx. To drink from the water, you must answer at least one of the sphinx's three questions correctly. If you drink from the oasis before asking the sphinx for permission, the sphinx captures you (all your muscles go limp) and gives you choice for doing maze or being eaten.

name: Bisaro
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Dalizi.

TODO

name: Black Hills
content:
Tags: mountains

The Black Hills outside Tephrakus contain huge mica deposits, are covered in large, basking lizards, and shadows that emit from the actual rock itself.

name: Blackpits
content:
Tags: swamp, geography, ve

A sprawling swamp in the north-central Dygon Beastlands, where dark water pools beneath a broken canopy and the dead do not always stay dead. The Blackpits occupy the lowest ground in the region—a basin where runoff from the surrounding jungle collects in stagnant pools of water so dark it reflects nothing.

The swamp stretches roughly sixty miles east to west and forty miles north to south, fed by seasonal flooding from a dozen streams that drain into the basin but never seem to drain out. Where the water goes is unclear; Ix'Meglyakuk legends speak of underground rivers, of water that descends to feed something far below.

name: Blackport
content:
Tags: citystate

Named after the black, lavarock bluffs the surround the island. A very convenient stop for traders from Mueras going south, it has grown to a large city. Built on a hillside, stacked on top of itself.

Also harboring some infamous pirates. The city is controlled by the retired, Nyolci pirate king Bridger Blindeyes who has decided to overlook the pirates who use his port.

Home to many Nyolci.

name: Bladewood
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Shyona.

TODO

name: Bleeding Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Mountain range within Emblydium, located to the north of the state's core territory.

TODO

name: Blistering Mountains
content: A mountain range north of Konai Mora, marking the northern boundary of the icy tundra.

name: Blisterswamp
content:
Tags: swamp, geography, ve, krell

A caustic wetland near the western edge of the Krell Lands, where geothermal activity creates bubbling pools of acidic water that nothing—not even the Krell—can tolerate. The swamp marks the border between Krell territory and Chimea's eastern claims.

name: Blood Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography, ve, chimea

The southernmost reach of the Widebarrow Mountains, the Blood Mountains take their name from the deep crimson iron ore veins that streak through the rock—and from the blood spilled extracting them. What should be Chimea's greatest treasure has become its most persistent drain.

name: Bloodlands
content:
Tags: region, geography

The scarred buffer zone between Atriik's southern reaches and the Ishnit Jungles, where generations of warfare between the Kor/Sharabha city-states and the Naruaghin tribes have stained the earth red—sometimes metaphorically, sometimes literally.

name: Bloodwood
content:
The forest west of the Apelands and north of Seyiy takes its name from its dominant tree species—the bloodwood, a hardwood with deep red heartwood that bleeds crimson sap when cut.

Bloodwood timber is extraordinarily valuable:
- Rot-resistant even in the wettest conditions
- Fire-resistant to temperatures that would char ordinary wood
- Beautiful when finished, with rich red grain patterns

The problem is harvesting it. Bloodwood forest sits between the Apelands to the north and the dangerous Seyiy highlands to the south. Logging expeditions must contend with ape incursions, the beasts of Seyiy, and the forest's own dangers. Most bloodwood that reaches markets comes from the Jungles of Titania in the Shacklands—a longer route but one with fewer forty-foot apes.

---

name: Bloodwood Forest

Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Shyona.

A forest in Wycendeula.

name: Blvnird

A city-state in Westrim.

name: Blyzrynthine
content: A canyonland region in the north of Pherezi Imnuttut, bordered by the Hills of Insanity to the north and east, and the mountainous region of Giantfell to the south. A fire leyline passes through Blyzrynthine, superheating the canyons below.

name: Bolsana P'Suelos
content:
Tags: region, geography

A region in Central Aboyinzu.

TODO

name: Bolsaña P'Suelos
content:
A southern forest whose name translates roughly as "bag of soils"—a reference to the unusual variety of ground conditions within its boundaries. Sandy patches border clay deposits border rocky outcrops, sometimes within the same clearing. Botanists find the forest fascinating; everyone else finds it annoying.

The varied soil produces varied flora, including several medicinal plants found nowhere else in the region. Herbalists from across Central Aboyinzu make pilgrimages to Bolsaña P'Suelos, though they often hire local guides—the forest's patchwork terrain makes navigation difficult.

---

name: Boneswamps
content:
A cursed swamp on the Chimea-Dern border, filled with the animated dead from the War of Bone (roughly 200 years ago). The bones of fallen Dernish and Chimean soldiers rise from the murky water, driven by residual hatred and the trauma of violent death. The skeletal warriors reenact their final battles, attacking any living creatures that enter their domain.

Neither nation claims the Boneswamps; both patrol their edges. The constant presence of undead makes this one of the most dangerous regions in Ve—and also the only reason Chimea and Dern aren't currently at war. The swamps serve as an impassable barrier.

name: Bonnetaz
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

Bonnetaz rises from the Salt Flats like a fever dream—towers of white salt-brick and bone, their surfaces carved with geometric patterns that catch the light. From a distance, the city appears beautiful. Up close, visitors notice that much of the "bone" ornamentation is human.

The Kuzagt have ruled here for over a thousand years, since before humans arrived in significant numbers on this continent. Their city predates the slave camps, but the camps have made it wealthy beyond measure.

name: Bragkur
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

TODO

name: Breathless Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography

A bay in L'Coth D'Hari.

TODO

name: Breidleheis
content:
Tags: region, giants

A high mountain basin ringed by the Gül Mountains—Ngül to the north, Egül to the east, Sorgül to the southeast—forming the heartland of the Güli giants. The name means "Broad Pasture" in the giants' tongue, and for millennia this sheltered valley has been their home.

name: Breidlheiss Basin
content:
The giant-inhabited basin east of Camaran, between Camaran and Adron. Camaran runs mining operations in the foothills, encroaching on giant territory while insisting the giants have no legitimate land claims. When giants retaliate, Camaran launches "defensive" expeditions and claims more land as security buffer.

The situation is morally grey: Camaran portrays giants as savage aggressors, but the aggression is largely retaliatory. Some Camarans who've dealt with the Ngül Mountain communities know giants can coexist peacefully with smaller folk—but admitting this would threaten mining interests.

name: Buffalo Islands
content:
Tags: island, geography

TODO

name: Buti
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Dalizi.

TODO

name: Büüli Umén
content: The capital, located in the central plains. Site of the great temple where the Kazigh rules, attended by servants selected through horse ritual and killed at age 30. The spiritual and political heart of Ta Minn.

name: Byonowidi
content:
Tags: city, state

A Sharabha city-state in western Atriik, the only non-Kor monarchy in the confederation. Located on the Sha Ulokoni near Sprenwood, Byonowidi is known for its fur trade and renowned cavalry.

See [Atriik](Atriik.md#byonowidi) for full details.

name: Byzkus River
content:
The Byzkus cuts through the heart of the Wilds, flowing south from somewhere near the Royon scrublands. It's the largest of the three—wide enough for canoes, fast enough to be dangerous, and long enough that no one knows its full course. Dwelyn hunters sometimes follow it inland. Most turn back before they learn what lives in the deeper stretches.

Since Surrey Mahaila's sickness began, the few hunters who venture along the Byzkus report strange things. Waters that glow wrong colors at night. Game that moves against the current. The sense that something is watching from just beneath the surface. Whether the river is connected to the Faesong poisoning, or merely reflects it, no one has stayed long enough to find out.

name: Caffas Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

A mountain range in the southern portion of the Sandreach Mountains system, positioned between the Grognil Mountains to the west and the coastal lowlands to the east. The Caffas are notable for the elemental leylines that run through them—both fire and force leylines cross here, and the region's inhabitants have adapted to harness that power.

name: Cañas
content:
Tags: city, capital

The capital city of Grustos.

TODO

name: Capital: Abyssward
content:
Built on the rim of the volcanic basin that contains the Pit of First Fire, Abyssward is a city that exists in constant awareness of the fire below.

**Population**: ~30,000

The city is smaller and poorer than the other Neferati capitals. Resources are scarce; the land doesn't support large populations. What Abyssward lacks in wealth, it makes up in intensity.

The architecture is stark—dark volcanic stone, minimal ornamentation, buildings designed to withstand earthquakes and eruptions. Beauty here is found in survival, not decoration.

The Pit dominates everything. Its glow lights the city at night. Its heat warms homes in winter. Its rumbling provides a constant background to life. The Wadiyans live above fire that could consume them at any moment—and this shapes who they are.

name: Capital: Ashenheart
content:
Yaif's capital is built into the slopes of an extinct volcano, its buildings carved from the same dark stone that surrounds it. The architecture is ancient—some structures predate recorded history—and the streets follow paths worn by thousands of years of feet.

**Population**: ~40,000

Ashenheart is a city of temples, shrines, and sacred sites. More priests per capita than any other city in Tarkhon. The spiritual heart of Neferati culture.

The city is quieter than outsiders expect. Where other Neferati kingdoms burn bright with passion and innovation, Yaif burns steady with tradition and memory. The fire here is ancient, patient, eternal.

name: Capital: Forgeheart
content:
Built around the Great Forge, Forgeheart is a city of artists, craftsmen, and those who serve them. Every street has workshops. Every plaza has galleries. The air smells of hot metal, exotic woods, and the oils used in painting.

**Population**: ~55,000

The city sprawls without plan—buildings added as needed over centuries, creating a maze of streets that locals navigate by landmark and outsiders navigate with difficulty. The architecture ranges from ancient stone structures to experimental designs that challenge conventional engineering.

Forgeheart is loud with the ring of hammers, the roar of furnaces, and the passionate arguments of artists about technique, meaning, and whether that particular shade of red truly captures the essence of flame.

name: Capital: Harborflame
content:
The largest city in the Kingdoms of Fire, built around the natural harbor protected by the Great Lighthouse. Harborflame is a bustling commercial center—warehouses, trading houses, shipyards, and markets that never close.

**Population**: ~80,000

The city is loud, crowded, and prosperous. Merchants from across Tarkhon and beyond come here to trade, despite the heat that makes non-Neferati miserable. The money is worth the discomfort.

Architecture in Harborflame is practical rather than beautiful—warehouses built for storage, homes built for comfort, temples that double as meeting halls. The one exception is the Lighthouse complex, which combines function with genuine grandeur.

name: Capital: Iara
content:
Iara is Breia's largest settlement and seat of governance, built in a sheltered valley in the eastern part of the state. The city developed around a cluster of hot springs heated by the fire leyline's proximity—providing year-round water and warmth that made permanent habitation possible.

The government of Breia operates from Iara, though "government" may be too strong a word. Breia is more a loose confederation of settlements that acknowledge Iara's leadership in matters of external trade and defense, while managing their own internal affairs.

name: Carcass Hills
content:
Tags: hills, ruins, geography

A range of low, bleached hills rising from the southern Fylvrae Sylvrym, where the forest thins before meeting the Kilbyurn foothills. The hills take their name from what lies beneath them—and from their appearance at the right angle.

name: Catalina
content: The capital city of Tornia, located on a river delta.

name: Cercesa Plains
content: The southeastern plains, between Büüli Umén and the coast. Less contested than the north, more pastoral.

name: Chálon Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest on Phenol.

Tags: dragon, creature

name: Cherruzil
content:
Tags: city, ruin

A ruined city-state on the Xeltri Plateau, destroyed by hill giants.

TODO

name: Cherry Blossom River Valley
content:
Tags: valley, foxborne

A beautiful valley and home to the Foxborne kingdom. Cliffs, pools of water, training grounds, watch towers, gardens, and butterflies abound.

name: Chilala
content:
Tags: state, elector, lakeshore circle

The largest state in the Dalizi Confederation—by population, by territory, by economic output, and by ambition. Chilala controls the eastern shore of Lake Tonactlet Chipe and dominates the Lakeshore Circle's politics. Its ruler, Regent-Queen Mwenekazi, is positioning to become the next Paramount, and everyone knows what that would mean: the end of the Confederation as a loose alliance, and the beginning of Chilala hegemony.

The state stretches from the lake shore into the highlands northeast, encompassing rich fishing grounds, productive farmland, and control of trade routes to the Elder Wilds. Its population exceeds the next three states combined. Its capital, Kisro, rivals Metato as the Confederation's largest city. Its armies—while less professional than Shunde's—can field more soldiers than any other member state.

Chilala's neighbors fear it. Chilala's allies benefit from its protection while resenting its dominance. The Confederation's political structure was designed to prevent any single state from becoming too powerful. Chilala is testing whether that design can hold.

name: Chitin Cove
content:
Tags: cove, geography, ve, krell, coast

The only safe landing point on the Krell Lands coast, Chitin Cove is named for the cast-off exoskeletons that wash up on its beaches—debris from Krell molting cycles carried downstream and deposited by coastal currents. Ships occasionally anchor here to trade with the survivors who've learned to live at the edge of insect territory.

name: Chitinwood
content:
East of the Phalkrene Mountains, the forest floor crunches underfoot. Chitinwood takes its name from what covers everything: the shed exoskeletons, the gnawed remains, the living shells of countless giant beetles.

This is not a civilization. There is no hive-mind, no beetle-society, no alien intelligence to negotiate with. Chitinwood is simply an ecosystem—one where beetles the size of horses are the dominant life form. Rhinoceros beetles that can gore through trees. Stag beetles whose mandibles shear through bone. Bombardier beetles that spray caustic chemicals. And countless smaller varieties that swarm, burrow, devour.

The beetles aren't aggressive by nature; they're simply beetles, doing what beetles do. But everything in Chitinwood has adapted to them. Predators have evolved chitin-cracking jaws. Prey have developed shells of their own. The trees grow thick, corky bark. Even the birds have beak-plates that can pry larvae from wood.

Travelers can pass through Chitinwood, but they must move carefully. Disturbing a beetle's territory, stumbling into a mating display, or simply smelling like food can trigger swarming behavior. The forest floor is littered with the picked-clean skeletons of those who made mistakes.

Chitin itself is valuable—beetle-plate armor, tool components, alchemical ingredients. Hunters from Pyonokraus occasionally venture into the edges of the forest to harvest, but they never go deep. Something larger lives in Chitinwood's interior. No one has seen it clearly. They've only found the shells it leaves behind—each one the size of a building.

name: Chykum
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Tarkhon.

TODO

name: Cirstin
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Cliffs of Shondromos
content:
Tags: cliffs, geography

A secondary cliff formation west of the Cliffs of Syquindonos—smaller but equally treacherous, marking where the Stratlan Sea meets the Western Isles. The Shondromos cliffs are known for their honeycombed structure: thousands of sea caves riddle the rock, creating a maze that smugglers, cultists, and stranger things call home.

name: Cliffs of Syquindonos
content:
Tags: cliffs, geography

A dramatic wall of stone marking the northern boundary of the Western Isles—sheer cliffs rising hundreds of feet from the Iron Sea, carved by millennia of waves and wind. The cliffs are as much barrier as landmark, separating the island region from the mainland coast beyond.

name: Constancias
content:
The capital of Qintas, a city of white stone and silver filigree built on terraces rising from the coastal plain. Constancias is beautiful in the way that only Amverela construction can be—every building positioned to catch light at optimal angles, every street aligned with sight lines to distant landmarks, every public space designed for contemplation.

The city is old. Not ancient in the way of ruins, but old in the way of living things that have simply continued existing. Buildings erected in ages past still house the same families. Gardens planted by elves long dead still bloom according to their original designs. The Amverela do not change Constancias; they maintain it.

Government is conducted through councils that meet rarely and decide slowly. Urgent matters are handled by delegated officials; truly urgent matters—war, plague, catastrophe—are handled by emergency procedures that have been invoked perhaps twice in living memory. The Amverela approach to crisis is to have planned for it centuries ago.

name: Copper Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Low, scrubby hills in western Kerwin, rich with copper ore. The hills provide Kerwin's modest mining industry and contain mysteries older than the halflings who work them.

name: Coquray Sound
content: The eastern coast opens into Coquray Sound, which connects to the larger Turquish Bay separating Edari from Shyona. The sound's waters are calmer than the open ocean, and Shyonan fishing vessels sometimes venture into its western reaches—though they rarely go ashore.

name: Crab Island
content:
Tags: island, geography

An island in the Agreben Sea.

name: Cragwood Swamp
content:
The poisoned heart of the Misty Marshes, where the catastrophe began. When Phendexelas first arrived and the fogs descended, this is where the swamp beasts went mad. Now, sixty years later, Cragwood is a nightmare zone that even the queen approaches with caution.

The creatures here are fog-touched—warped by decades of concentrated exposure into something between animal and aberration. Fog Crocodiles breathe mist instead of air and can phase partially through solid matter, their jaws materializing inside a victim's chest. Marsh Stalkers were once deer-like grazers; now they hunt in packs on too many legs, fog leaking from eyes that glow with dim intelligence. And in the deepest pools lurk the Old Ones—what the swamp's apex predators became when the madness took them. Massive, patient, and intelligent in ways they should not be, the Old Ones remember what they were before and hate what they've become.

The queen built Maurevelious deliberately close to Cragwood's edge. She wants her subjects to see the horrors beyond the walls, to understand viscerally what waits for them outside her protection. Occasionally, she sends dissidents into Cragwood for "meditation." None have returned sane.

name: Crystal Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A fifty-mile spine of peaks running northwest to southeast through Central Aboyinzu, separating Terrenia and the horrors of Chaal Nazzerox from the forests of M'Svyla and the southern grasslands. The range takes its name from the dense quartz formations that stud its slopes—on clear days, the peaks catch the sun and throw light across the foothills in blinding sheets.

name: Cydoris River
content:
Tags: river, geography

A seasonal lifeline through dying hills.

The Cydoris River flows through the southwestern Hills of Dolor, emerging from springs in the high ground near the Widebarrow Mountains and winding northeast toward Gnotobi's lake. Along with its tributary the Marshal River, it provides the primary water source for the western desert region and ultimately feeds the Golden River system.

name: Da Sharma
content:
The southern region of Kerwin, where farmland gives way to sheep pastures and the influence of Tarkha fades. Da Sharma is insular, traditional, and faintly suspicious of outsiders—including halflings from northern Kerwin.

The people here maintain old shrines to spirits that predate Tarkhon, predate Kerwin, predate perhaps the halflings themselves. They light candles on certain nights, leave offerings at certain stones, and speak certain names only in whispers. When asked about these practices, they smile and change the subject.

name: Da Trang
content:
Tags: state, nation

Da Trang controls the northern reaches of the Jungles of Godahi, a territory defined by its position between the Green Mountains and the deeper jungle to the south. The nation commands the jungle interior and the vital mountain passes connecting the Godahi basin to the coastal settlements around Micah Inlet.

name: Daboni
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Dalibela
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Daltimo
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Dappled Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Shyona.

TODO

name: Dar Qisu
content:
Tags: citystate

An independent city-state. The name suggests desert or Neferati cultural influence.

TODO: Determine location, develop government, culture, economy, and notable features.

name: Darkstone Caves
content:
Located approximately one hundred miles south of Kyorda, deep beneath the Denrik Sea at the intersection of earth and dark leylines, lies an extensive underwater cave system known only as the Darkstone Caves. This is the sole known source of darkstone in all of Alaria—a rare mineral that emanates a natural aura of darkness and resists all geomantic manipulation unless the wielder is paragon-attuned to darkness itself.

The caves are perpetually shrouded in supernatural shadow that cannot be dispelled by normal light sources. The leyline convergence creates zones of absolute darkness where even magical sight fails. Currents within the underwater tunnels are unpredictable and deadly, formed by the interaction between earth magic stabilizing the cave structure and dark magic warping space within.

Mining darkstone requires specialized diving equipment, dark-attuned mages to navigate the lightless depths, and extraordinary courage. Most who attempt the descent never return—claimed by the crushing pressure, the disorienting darkness, or the strange creatures that have adapted to live in a realm of eternal shadow beneath the waves.

name: Deadjack Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography, dangerous

Deadjack Forest is a dense woodland in southeastern Kadroka, positioned between the Fury Hills and the approaches to the Agony Stones. The forest is named for the jack pines that dominate it—trees that appear dead, with bare grey branches, but are very much alive and deeply unsettling.

name: Deadman's Lake
content:
Tags: lake, geography, poi

A cold mountain lake nestled in the northern Kilbyurn foothills, where the forest begins to thin and the terrain rises toward the high peaks. The lake's waters are clear and deep, fed by snowmelt and underground springs, with a surface that reflects the surrounding mountains with mirror-like clarity.

The name comes from what floats in it.

name: Deadwater Bay
content:
At the peninsula's southern tip, Deadwater Bay earns its name. Nothing lives here. The water is clear—too clear, sterile as glass. No fish swim in it. No seaweed clings to its rocks. No barnacles grow on the few wrecks that have drifted in. Even bacteria seem absent; corpses that wash into the bay do not rot. They simply... remain.

The effect extends several hundred yards from shore. Sailors who must pass through report an oppressive silence, a sense of being watched, and a wrongness they can't articulate. Some claim the water itself feels different—heavier, more reluctant to move.

No one knows what killed Deadwater Bay, or what keeps it dead. The Zelle avoid it. Pyonokraus ignores it. Even Thrallwood's compulsion seems to have no interest in this stretch of coast. Whatever happened here was thorough, and whatever lingers here is patient.

name: Desendrum Mountains
content:
Tags: hills, mountains, geography

A rugged north-south ridge in the central Westwilds, the Desendrum range marks the transition between the forested west and the open plains to the east. Despite the name "mountains," the Desendrum are modest by Alarian standards—more accurately described as a highland spine of worn peaks and steep-sided valleys. Locals call them the Desendrum Hills with equal frequency.

name: Deserted Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in Gorath.

Mountain range within Emblydium, located to the south of the state's core territory.

Mountains on Apketrii.

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

name: Divinity Passage
content:
Tags: strait, waterway, trade route

The main maritime highway connecting Gorath to the outside world—and the primary route for the Tamadrez slave trade. The Passage cuts between the Gorathi mainland and the scattered islands to the south, providing sheltered waters for the endless traffic of merchant vessels, slave ships, and naval patrols that keep the empire's economy moving.

The name is ancient, predating Gorath itself. Local legend claims the passage was carved by a god fleeing some primordial catastrophe, though which god and what catastrophe varies by telling. The Gorathi have adopted the name without the mythology—to them, it simply describes the route that brings divine favor in the form of endless labor.

name: Dnykuul
content:
Tags: hills, geography

A rugged hill country in the Sandreach Mountains, positioned between Breia to the north and the Scarlet Peaks to the east. The Dnykuul hills are dry, rocky, and largely barren—scrub vegetation, exposed stone, and seasonal streams that run dry for most of the year.

name: Dodi

Tags: city, capital

The capital city of Dern.

A point of interest in the United Free Nations.
