name: Dragon's Teeth
content:
Tags: island, geography

A group of islands in the Western Isles.

A region of more, treacherous terrain. Extremely sharp rocks made of obsidian stick up from the water, which can shred a boat, and definitely your body. Mysterious currents surround the area, and the Qorgath lives somewhere in its murky depths, guarding her hatch of Erzqin.

The Erzqin also patrol the waters, slaying and destroying those who dare to enter.

name: Drakwald Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography, rakite, dangerous

A dense woodland in the southwestern corner of Rakite, where the Tyror Weklis plains meet the Vokas Pendi foothills. The Rakiten don't enter it. They don't talk about why.

name: Draphilir
content:
Tags: dragon, creature, ve, green

A green dragon who has claimed the eastern Dygon Beastlands—the region called Blyss—as her domain for over five centuries. Draphilir is patient, calculating, and profoundly territorial. Where her clutch-sibling Pyaganos rules through visibility and fear, Draphilir rules through rot, disease, and the certainty that she is always watching from somewhere you cannot see.

name: Draphilir's Domain
content:
The dragon Draphilir has claimed Blyss for over five centuries. Her influence pervades the region.

**The Green Rot:** Draphilir's breath weapon—a cloud of corrosive, spore-laden vapor—has been deployed enough times over the centuries that its effects linger. Certain areas of Blyss harbor permanent concentrations of the dragon's exhalations. These "rot pockets" accelerate decay dramatically; fresh meat becomes bone in hours, living tissue exposed to the vapors develops necrotic infections. The Ix'Meglyakuk mark rot pockets with specific warning signs and detour around them.

**Tribute Zones:** Draphilir demands regular tribute from Ix'Meglyakuk tribes whose territories overlap her domain. Every three months, designated offerings—meat, precious stones, interesting artifacts—must be left at specific clearings. The dragon collects these at her leisure. Tribes that fail to provide tribute are hunted. The system has operated for generations; no one living remembers a time before it.

**The Lair:** Draphilir's primary lair is somewhere in central Blyss, its exact location known only to a few Ix'Meglyakuk elders who maintain necessary contact with the dragon. By all accounts, it occupies a massive sinkhole where an underground river surfaces briefly before diving back beneath the jungle floor. The cavern system is extensive, flooded in places, and filled with centuries of accumulated treasure.

name: Dreamburn Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Singa Chike, Dalizi.

TODO

name: Dreske Gemalis
content:
Tags: mountains, river, geography

A rugged mountain range marking the western border of Nashua, where the Inner Rim transitions from the highland territories to the toxic lowlands. The name applies both to the peaks themselves and to the cold, mineral-rich river that flows from them into Nashuan territory.

name: Drigard Gakh
content:
Tags: city, ruin

A ruined city-state on the Xeltri Plateau, destroyed by hill giants.

TODO

name: Droaz River
content:
Tags: river, geography

A river in Gorath.

TODO

name: Droughd Sound
content:
Tags: sound, geography, water

The gateway to Gnotobi's empire.

Droughd Sound is the central arm of Turquish Bay, a sheltered inlet on Ve's northern coast where the Golden River empties into the sea. Orangeport occupies its southern shore, making this body of water the funnel through which virtually all Orange Flake exports pass on their way to the wider world.

name: Dryenthia Valley
content:
The Dryenthia Valley cuts through the heart of Argysis, running roughly east-west between the mountain peaks. Before the conquest, this valley housed farms, orchards, and the small settlements that fed the great cities. Now it is largely abandoned—the population consolidated into defensible positions closer to Othyndria.

The valley floor is still fertile, but few dare work it. Rothogomos patrols his domain from above, and anything that catches his attention tends to be eaten.

name: Dunes of Evioli
content:
Tags: desert, dunes, geography, region

A sea of sand hiding an empire of orange.

The Dunes of Evioli stretch across north-central Ve, a vast desert of rolling sand dunes that shifts color from pale gold to deep amber depending on the light. At its heart lies Gnotobi's lake—the source of Orange Flake and the reason anyone cares about this otherwise hostile expanse.

name: Dunes of Kalamesh
content:
Tags: desert, geography

A vast expanse of towering sand dunes in the northeastern Luquihn Desert, stretching inland from the Sunshine Coast. The dunes here reach heights of several hundred feet—massive, shifting mountains of pale gold sand that reshape themselves with every windstorm.

The Kalamesh dunes are notoriously difficult to navigate. Landmarks disappear overnight as the sands shift, and the dunes create disorienting echo effects that confuse travelers. Local guides use the stars and the position of distant mountain peaks to navigate, but even experienced desert-dwellers treat the Kalamesh with caution.

Beneath the deepest dunes, rumors persist of buried ruins—remnants of a civilization that existed before the desert expanded to its current boundaries. Whether these ruins connect to the underground Murazi settlements is unknown; the Murazi themselves are secretive about what lies beneath the sands.

name: Durnigan Highlands
content:
Tags: highlands, geography

Highlands in Taulo Magi, Dalizi.

TODO

name: Duskwood Forest
content:
A dense forest in southwestern Phirexes named for the permanent twilight under its canopy. This is not a fog effect—the trees here grow so thick, their leaves so broad and overlapping, that sunlight simply cannot penetrate. The forest floor exists in eternal dusk, cool and dim and silent.

This darkness nurtures things that grow nowhere else. Fungi that glow with bioluminescence in colors the Dengar have names for but other languages don't. Mosses that can be processed into medicines for ailments other healers cannot touch. Flowers that bloom only in absolute darkness, their petals worth their weight in gold to alchemists who know their uses.

Before the queen came, Duskwood was where the Dengar's greatest alchemists gathered their rarest ingredients. Now, entry requires royal permission. Phendexelas controls the medicine supply by controlling access to what makes it. Petitions to harvest in Duskwood are granted sparingly, and always with a price—usually information about one's neighbors.

name: Dustforge
content:
Tags: citystate, settlement

A city-state in the southeastern Luquihn Desert, built around Esedin Lake—a large oasis fed by underground aquifers. Dustforge is one of the few permanent settlements in the Sandreach, made possible by reliable water access and ingenious architectural adaptation to the brutal desert environment.

name: Dustwind Plateau
content:
Tags: plains, geography, arid

The arid heart of Mueras, a high tableland swept by winds that never cease. The Dustwind Plateau separates the wealthy coastal cities from the eastern reaches—a buffer zone where few settlements survive and fewer prosper. Caravans cross it to avoid the longer sea route around the Beak, but the journey is harsh, and the plateau claims its share of the unprepared.

The plateau rises gradually from the coast, reaching its full height some forty miles inland. From there, it stretches east to the edge of the Taflex Forest, north to the Mueras Highlands, and south toward the approaches to Grimreach. The elevation creates the dryness—moisture from the sea rarely reaches this far—and the constant wind strips what little water remains.

name: Dylania
content:
The capital and largest city in Eoga. Dylania occupies one of the few large areas of solid ground in the nation, a rocky outcrop rising above the southern jungle near the Cloud Mountain foothills. The city is built vertically, climbing the slopes of its foundation rock in tiers of wooden buildings connected by stairs and rope bridges.

Dylania controls access to the Cloud Mountain passes and extracts tolls from all traffic heading south. This geographic chokepoint is the primary source of Eoga's revenue and political relevance.

name: East Naruaghin Tribes
content:
Tags: state, nation

The East Naruaghin occupy the borderlands between the Ishnit Jungles and the Green Wilds of Enapay. Once the most prosperous of the three tribal confederations, they are now locked in an existential war with the Dwelyn—a conflict they were losing badly until Surrey Mahaila fell ill.

name: Eberri Ygonzi
content:
Tags: forest, geography, dangerous, monsters, wycendeula

The great forest of southwestern Wycendeula—a dense expanse of ancient conifers and tangled undergrowth stretching from the Neurian Hills to the edge of Kama Sa'malina. Where Eyendra in the northeast is haunted by old magic, Eberri Ygonzi is simply, brutally wild. This is old growth that has never been logged, never been settled, never been tamed. The things that live here have had thousands of years to become very good at killing.

The name is archaic—no one remembers what language it comes from or what it originally meant. Locals sometimes call it the Ygonzi or just "the deep forest." Whatever you call it, the message is the same: don't go in.

name: Edkovic
content:
The capital, if Enavadi can be said to have one. Edkovic is the largest settlement—perhaps five thousand souls—but it feels like an overgrown fishing village that happens to host the government. No walls protect it. No monuments celebrate past victories. The architecture is practical: wooden buildings clustered around an enormous harbor complex, everything oriented toward the sea.

The **Great Docks** stretch for miles along the waterfront—a maze of wooden piers, boathouses, and chandleries organized by an elaborate system of family berths that outsiders find incomprehensible. Each extended family maintains hereditary rights to specific dock sections, passed down through generations. Disputes over berth boundaries have occasionally ended in violence, though the net-councils usually resolve such matters before blood is shed.

The **Smoking Halls** dominate the town's interior—massive communal facilities for preserving fish. The smell defines Edkovic. Visitors notice it immediately and never entirely stop noticing. The smoke-scent clings to hair, clothes, and skin for days after departure. Locals claim they can identify which village a person comes from by the particular character of smoke in their clothes.

The **Stranger's Quarter** occupies a single district where outsiders may stay, eat, and conduct trade. It is clean, adequate, and utterly without warmth. A low stone wall—not defensive, merely symbolic—separates it from the rest of Edkovic. Outsiders who venture beyond this boundary are not physically prevented, but they are watched, followed, and made to understand they are not welcome.

The **Empty Temple** stands near the Stranger's Quarter—a stone building with no god's name carved above its door. A missionary from the Middle Sea raised it three centuries ago, convinced he could bring foreign faith to these simple fishing folk. The Enavadi let him build it. They never entered. It has been a warehouse for dried nets for two hundred years, and the missionary's name has been forgotten.

name: Egül Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, giants

The eastern arm of the Gül Mountains, separating the Breidleheis basin from the broader Kharvorn range. The Egül peaks are the tallest and most rugged of the Gül ranges, forming an imposing wall that has long protected the giants from eastern incursions.

name: Egzahd
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

TODO

name: El Djele
content:
Tags: citystate

An independent city-state. The name suggests desert or Neferati cultural influence.

TODO: Determine location, develop government, culture, economy, and notable features.

name: Elaphant Groves
content:
Tags: forest, grove, geography

Groves in Old Tolaria.

TODO

name: Elder Mountains
content: The **Elder Mountains** rise at the southeastern corner of the Dalizi Highlands, a weathered range of ancient stone that connects the Foggy Mountains to the coastal cliffs above the Shipwreck Coast. The mountains take their name from their geological age—these are among the oldest exposed formations in Aboyinzu, predating the upheavals that created the younger ranges to the west.

name: Elderran
content:
Tags: city, ruins, magical-disaster, old-tolaria

The ruined capital of Old Tolaria, and the epicenter of one of the most catastrophic magical disasters in Alarian history. What remains is not merely a ruin—it is an ongoing unraveling of reality itself, where time, elements, and the fundamental nature of magic have been torn apart and left bleeding.

name: Elephant Groves
content:
Tags: forest, sacred, region

The Elephant Groves are the largest forest in the region—a vast, ancient woodland that serves as the birthplace and spiritual homeland of the loxodon race. The forest is sacred ground, protected by an ancient elephant spirit that does not welcome uninvited visitors.

name: Elessi Bay
content: The main bay on Eloesi's western coast, sheltering Kair and several smaller settlements. The waters are deep enough for ocean-going vessels, and the harbor never freezes. Most trade passes through here.

name: Elorshin Grera
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Lenora.

TODO

name: Ember Isles
content:
Tags: archipelago, geography

A volcanic island chain in the southwestern Western Isles, where the earth still burns and ancient Neferati experiments left scars that haven't healed. The islands glow with inner heat, shrouded in steam and ash, home to things that thrive in fire.

name: Emtus
content:
A crater lake in the Celedrim Plains.

TODO

name: Enavadi
content:
Tags: state, nation, fishing

A stretch of rocky coastline and sheltered bays along the Seacleft Coast, home to the most skilled fishermen in Alaria. Enavadi is less a unified nation than a confederation of fishing villages bound by shared customs, intermarriage, and a deep suspicion of outsiders. The land is poor—thin soil, few resources, no strategic value—but the waters are rich, and the Enavadi have built everything around them.

The coast runs roughly north-south, with Edko Sound and the Floss Islands forming a natural harbor complex in the north, and Prince's Bay opening to the south toward the waters around Gyosha. Between them lie a handful of fishing villages, each with its own specialty and character, collectively producing catches that feed markets across the Seacleft Coast.

name: Enuau Kemjallb
content:
Tags: citystate

The radiant House of the Sun—a golden city-state of the Kryaaji sun elves, forbidden to outsiders. Enuau Kemjallb rises from the shores of Sopona Fia lake in western Grustos, its white stone towers and golden domes reflecting sunlight in displays visible for miles. The Light leyline passes directly through the city, and the Kryaaji believe this is no coincidence.

The sun elves maintain strict isolation not from arrogance but from sacred duty. They believe their light and their home are too pure for outsiders to touch—that contact with the wider world would diminish what they guard. Visitors are turned away at the boundary markers, politely but with spears ready. The Lightbearer priests who patrol these borders radiate actual sunlight from their eye sockets, a reminder that the Kryaaji's faith is not merely symbolic.

Trade with the outside world occurs at designated boundary markets along the Flejdeaako Yni river, never within the city itself. Merchants leave goods at appointed places; the elves collect what interests them and leave payment. No haggling. No conversation. The system works because Kryaaji craftsmanship commands premium prices, and their Wysynnbre-trained eagles are beyond price entirely.

name: Erdel
content:
Tags: citystate

Use water and jungle plants to make dyes. The processing horribly pollutes the water and completley poisoned the Zodine river. Killed off Donokasi.

name: Erdene
content: The capital city, seat of both the House of Nobles and the House of Commerce. A sprawling trade hub where deals are struck in every tavern, market, and noble house. The architecture reflects centuries of accumulated wealth—ornate but practical, designed to impress clients.

name: Eshire Moors
content:
Tags: hills, moors, geography

Rolling moorlands in the Wanderlands.

TODO

name: Ethundra
content:
South of Withepest, where the steppe begins to transition toward the forested foothills of the Kharvorn Mountains, lies the region called Ethundra—a borderland of scrubby vegetation, frozen marshes, and the first scattered stands of hardy conifers that eventually thicken into the Winterwood.

Ethundra is marginally more hospitable than Withepest proper. Small game survives here—arctic hares, foxes, ptarmigan—and the Zwaeron sometimes establish temporary camps in the more sheltered valleys. But the region is contested territory, with Jotun hunting parties occasionally ranging down from the Jotunhills to raid Zwaeron camps and carry off prisoners for their frozen larders.

name: Eynmore
content:
The Rhea capital on Mt. Disyus. A city of contemplation, scholarship, and mastery.

Eynmore doesn't feel like a city to human visitors—it's too quiet, too orderly, too empty of the bustle that defines urban life. The Rhea don't crowd together; they space themselves for focus. The result is a settlement that seems half-populated even when everyone is present.

**Architecture**: Carved from mountain stone, elegant but austere. Buildings are integrated into the rock rather than imposed upon it. Open spaces allow air to flow—the Rhea need to feel the wind.

**Government**: The **High Contemplative** leads, currently **Whisper-of-Still-Waters**—a Rhea so old and focused that she barely acknowledges the physical world. She speaks rarely. When she does, Eynmore listens.

**Character**: Quiet. Patient. Dedicated to pursuits measured in decades. Visitors find it peaceful or maddening depending on temperament.

name: Ezra's Temptation
content:
Tags: river, geography

A winding river that flows through the eastern portion of Milrar Forest before emerging onto the Steppe of Aziirn, named for a legendary explorer whose story serves as both cautionary tale and local joke.

name: Faewoods
content:
Tags: forest, magical, faesong, old-tolaria

A forest in Old Tolaria that should not exist. The Faewoods were not grown—they were created in an instant when the Apparatus of Severance at Elderran attempted to cage the Faesong, and the Faesong exploded outward instead.

name: Falsehaven Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography

A broad, sheltered bay on Central Aboyinzu's western coast, where Satyr Wood meets the sea. The name dates back centuries, to a time when ships still occasionally sailed these waters and learned—too late—that the bay's shelter came with costs.

name: Fantasy Lakes
content: Home to many rusalka and dwarven skeletons. Lots of loot at the bottom of the lakes.

name: Farthing Inlet
content:
Tags: bay, geography

TODO

name: Fernius Sound
content:
Tags: sea, geography

The body of water east of Petrona Septia Island, separating the island from the scattered archipelagos and open waters beyond. Fernius Sound is broad, deep, and relatively calm — better sailing than the hazardous waters around the Tyvern Isles to the north or the shallow approaches to Phylos Bay in the west.

The sound sees moderate traffic: fishing boats from the Petrona Septia settlements, occasional merchant vessels heading to or from Rilyn, and the rare ship making for the more remote eastern destinations. It's not a major trade route — Petrona Septia's states don't generate enough commerce for that — but it's navigable and relatively safe.

name: Fields of Winter
content:
Tags: plains, geography

TODO

name: Finori
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Firefell
content:
Tags: poi, ruins

The shattered remains of an ancient Neferati colony in the Ember Isles—a city that tried to harness volcanic power and paid the price. Now half-submerged in cooling lava, haunted by fire elementals, and guarded by secrets the Kingdoms of Fire would kill to control.

name: Fireknife Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Part of the greater Wurmspine range, the Fireknife is a volcanic segment along the northwestern border of Kerwin, marking the boundary between settled Tarkhon territory and the wild lands beyond. Dangerous, unstable, and largely avoided by sensible travelers.

name: Fleimrut Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

Extremely treacherous. Underground fire-geysers are all over the place. Lots of caves, equally treacherous.

name: Floating Islands of Aleve Akribus
content:
Tags: island, geography, magical

A cluster of islands that hang suspended in the air above the sea, southwest of the Torguli Islands chain. The Floating Islands of Aleve Akribus drift at altitudes ranging from fifty to several hundred feet, their positions shifting slowly with winds that don't match the currents below. From a distance, they appear as dark shapes against the sky — obviously solid, obviously not where islands should be.

No one knows why they float. The islands have hung in the air for as long as anyone remembers, and theories about their origin range from ancient magic to leyline anomalies to something buried in the islands' cores that defies natural law. Whatever the cause, the effect is undeniable: these islands do not touch the water.

name: Forest of Avarice
content:
Tags: forest, geography, dangerous, fae

A dense, ancient woodland marking the eastern boundary of Echem Yiakraxes, the Forest of Avarice earned its name from the prospectors and treasure-hunters who enter seeking fortune and emerge—if they emerge—empty-handed and changed. The Terrogones who dominate the plains refuse to hunt beneath its canopy. Local Aboyinzan peoples consider this the most damning warning possible: if apex predators won't go there, neither should you.

name: Forest of Esperanza
content:
The northern reaches of Azawahasi give way to the Forest of Esperanza, a dense woodland that marks the transition between savanna and the more temperate regions beyond. The forest is old, its trees tall and closely spaced, providing shelter from the sun and wind that dominates the open plains.

Some Sharabha tribes range into Esperanza's edges, hunting the forest game that differs from their usual savanna prey. The forest offers different challenges—dense undergrowth, limited sightlines, prey that hides rather than runs. Tribes that specialize in forest hunting have adapted their techniques accordingly.

The deeper forest remains largely unexplored by the Sharabha. They're plains people at heart, uncomfortable in enclosed spaces where their lion forms lose their advantage. Whatever lives in Esperanza's interior has been left alone.

name: Fozyader
content:
The capital of Roule sprawls along the northeastern coast where the Arins Plains meet the sea. White stone buildings with red tile roofs climb gentle hills, and the harbor is a forest of masts. This is where the Honey Lords meet, where colonial policy is made, and where Roule's wealth accumulates in counting houses and vaults.

The city is beautiful in the way wealthy places often are—clean streets, manicured parks, impressive public buildings. The Parliament of Flowers convenes in a domed hall at the city's heart, though real power rests with the merchant councils that meet in private clubs nearby. Halfling society here is elaborate and status-obsessed, with subtle gradations of rank indicated by hat styles, flower pins, and seating positions at public events.

Fozyader's darker business happens in the Harbor District and the warren of streets behind the main markets. Slave auctions occur in private courtyards. Captains can be hired for any cargo, no questions asked. The city watch is efficient but selective about what it sees.

Foreign visitors find Fozyader welcoming on the surface—good food, comfortable inns, polite conversation. But there's a coldness beneath the hospitality, a sense that outsiders are being assessed for usefulness. The halflings smile, and the smiles don't reach their eyes.

name: Fragenstor Mielthøn
content:
Tags: forest, tangiern, crimson-coast

The great forest of Tangiern—hundreds of square miles of dark conifer woodland covering most of the human kingdom's territory. The name is Old Tangier for "Forest of a Thousand Thoughts," and the forest earns it. Those who travel too deep report strange mental states: vivid dreams, borrowed memories, conversations with people who aren't there.

name: Free Colonies
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state on Ayashk Island.

TODO

name: Freshwater Sound
content:
A large bay or sound dominating the northwestern coast, providing Qintas with access to maritime trade. The waters are calm, protected from ocean storms by the peninsula that curves around from Grustos, making Freshwater Sound one of the safest harbors in Central Aboyinzu.

The Amverela were never great sailors—they prefer the patience of land-based pursuits to the unpredictability of the sea—but they've always been willing to let others do the sailing. Freshwater Sound's ports welcome traders from across Alaria, all eager to purchase Sondoros wine and whatever else the high elves deign to sell.

name: Frowning Forest
content:
A twisted woodland in the east where the trees grow in downward-curving shapes, their branches drooping like disapproving brows. Local legend claims the forest watched something terrible happen and has worn the expression ever since.

The Frowning Forest is not actively hostile like Spiritwood or Mournful Wood—it's simply oppressive. The trees block light. Sound carries strangely. Travelers report the constant sensation of being observed and judged. Most pass through as quickly as possible, though "quickly" is relative when every path seems to curve back on itself.

Refugees have learned to use the Frowning Forest as a hiding place. The undead can navigate it, but they're slow and easily confused by the twisting paths. A living person who knows the routes can stay ahead of pursuit for days.

---

name: Further Sound
content:
The open water east of Phyr Island, connecting to the Sea of Fools to the south. Further Sound is relatively safe—the curse's effects diminish rapidly east of the Statue Hills. Ships approaching Attla come this way, and the sound sees occasional traffic from traders willing to deal with the islanders.

The name reflects the sound's position: further from Ve's mainland than any other named water, at the edge of the known world before the poisoned beauty of the Sea of Fools begins.

name: Fynol
content: A fishing and raiding settlement on Tykos's southern edge. The clans here specialize in coastal raids—fast ships, quick strikes, captured populations. They supply raw material to Slavos's markets and take a percentage of the sale price.

name: Fyrsan Al Troikas
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A jagged mountain range forming the eastern boundary of Satyr Wood, running roughly north-south for over a hundred miles. The name is old—possibly Spine Goblin in origin, possibly older—and translates approximately to "the teeth that bite back." The mountains have earned the name. They're treacherous for everyone, including the goblins who call them home.

name: Geordon
content: The capital, in the center of Thespia. Currently under Akta's erratic rule. The royal palace has become her seat of power—and reportedly a site of strange magical experiments.

name: Gesichi Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Mountains on Pesalolo.

TODO

name: Geyser Bay
content: A bay on the eastern coast where Siquestrya meets the sea. The bay takes its name from the thermal vents that dot the coastline and shallow waters, sending plumes of steam and mineral-rich water into the air at irregular intervals. The geysers make navigation treacherous for larger vessels, but the warm waters attract unusual marine life and the mineral deposits have modest value to alchemists. The centaur tribes of Siquestrya generally avoid this coastline, considering the geysers ill-omened.

name: Gherdikhisar
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Giant's Corridor
content:
Tags: mountain range

The Giant's Corridor is a passage between two mountain ranges. It is the passage through which the nomadic, pastoral giant herders bring their flocks.

name: Giants Cradle
content:
Tags: valley, sacred-site, giants, geography

A bowl-shaped valley nestled between the Koilos and Myjornis mountain ranges, containing two sacred lakes central to giant spiritual tradition. This is where giants believe their race was born, and where they come to die.

name: Gilded Coast
content:
Tags: coast, geography

A coastal region on Ofrenia.

TODO

name: Gleiwood Forest
content: Forest in the east, inland from the coast. Managed for timber—Dwarblin shipyards have an endless appetite for wood.

name: Glezamos
content:
The current capital of Zelidia sprawls across both banks of the Sovia, connected by bridges woven from living vines. Glezamos was built hastily after the fall of Iirmos, and its architecture still carries that desperate, improvised quality—structures that grew organically around the needs of refugees rather than following any grand design.

The Temple of the Hungry Canopy dominates the city's center, where the High Priestesses conduct their sacrifices and commune with the jungle-spirits. Foreign delegations (the rare few permitted to live) are received in the Bleeding Court, an open plaza where the stones are permanently stained rust-red.

name: Glidwyn Forest
content:
Tags: forest, region

Glidwyn Forest fills the transition zone between Eloweir coastal territory and the interior plains, a dense temperate woodland that marks where Eloesi ends and wilder lands begin. The forest isn't claimed by any nation—it's simply there, a green buffer between the sea elves and the goblin chaos to the east.

name: Glory River
content:
Tags: river, geography, ve, sestros

The sacred waterway of Sestros, flowing from the foothills of the Shepherds' Stones through the kingdom's heartland. The Shailin believe water that touches Talresses's chosen land carries his blessing—and since Talresses has chosen all of Sestros, the Glory River is considered especially holy.

name: Glyn'Qoth
content:
Tags: island, geography

TODO

name: Gnarwood Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Old Tolaria.

TODO

name: Gnotobi
content:
Tags: citystate, settlement

The orange city. Gnotobi sprawls along the shores of its vast lake in the Dunes of Evioli, a haze of refinement smoke hanging over its warehouses and the sweet-chemical smell of processing hanging in the air. This is the only place in the world where Orange Flake is made—and that monopoly has made Gnotobi obscenely wealthy.

The lake itself dominates everything. Miles across, shallow, warm, and carpeted with the distinctive orange algae that grows nowhere else. From the surrounding dunes, the water looks like molten copper at sunset. Up close, it smells of salt and something faintly medicinal.

name: Goataya
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills within Shyona.

TODO

name: Golden River
content:
Tags: river, geography, trade

The most profitable waterway in Ve.

The Golden River flows north from Gnotobi's lake through the Dunes of Evioli, emptying into Droughd Sound at Orangeport. This single river carries virtually every dose of Orange Flake that leaves the desert—a stream of narcotic wealth that has made fortunes, broken treaties, and shaped the politics of an entire region.

name: Goldwood
content:
Tags: forest, geography, ve, avalon

A sacred forest in western Avalon, between the settled heartland and Munari Inlet. The trees here have distinctive golden-yellow bark that catches afternoon light, making the forest seem to glow from within. The Temptari consider Goldwood sacred to the aspect of time governing autumn and endings.

name: Grailwood Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Old Tolaria.

TODO

name: Gray Isles
content:
Tags: island, geography

Islands in the Gray Sea.

TODO

name: Graystone
content:
Tags: city, capital

The capital city of Balduahr.

TODO

name: Greenskin Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

TODO

name: Grieb Lakes
content:
Tags: lake, geography

A series of freshwater lakes in the hills of the northeastern Wildwood, home to the Griebi people. The largest is Cykravikus Lake; smaller lakes dot the surrounding hills.

name: Grimreach
content: The eastern frontier of Mueras, where the Taflex Forest gives way to the rocky, fog-shrouded coastline above the Darkened Coast. Grimreach is contested territory—nominally part of Mueras, but the merchant-kings' authority fades rapidly this far from the Sound. The settlements here deal with shadow-touched trade goods, monster parts, and dark magic components that the respectable ports won't handle openly.

name: Grognil Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A rugged mountain range in the southwestern Sandreach Mountains system, positioned between the Caffas Mountains to the east and the lowlands descending toward Wycendeula. The Grognil range is older and more weathered than its neighbors, with rounded peaks and deep valleys carved by ancient glaciers.

name: Grothak
content: The capital, built into a mountainside. Partially evacuated—the wealthy fled south with their gold, leaving the poor to fend for themselves. The lower districts are dangerous; silvertongue incursions happen weekly. What remains of the government operates from fortified upper levels.

name: Grove of Migwam
content:
"Migwam" in the old Dengar tongue means "grandmother" or "wise mother." Before the queen came, this grove in the northern reaches of Old One's Wood was the Dengar's holiest site—a ring of ancient grandmother-trees where ancestors' ashes were scattered and their spirits were said to linger. Dengar would make pilgrimages here to seek guidance, and the ancestors would answer through rustling leaves and prophetic dreams.

Now the queen has claimed the grove as a shrine to herself. She visits monthly, performing rituals she claims channel the ancestors' blessing upon her reign. The grandmother-trees have been hung with silk banners bearing her image, and an altar of white stone stands at the grove's center where she receives "the ancestors' wisdom."

But the ancestors have gone silent. Since the fogs came, no pilgrim has received a true vision at Migwam. The leaves do not rustle with messages. The dreams are empty. The refugees believe the ancestors have withdrawn in disgust—or that the queen's presence has driven them away entirely. Some fear something worse: that the fogs have trapped the ancestors too, binding them as they bind the living.

name: Grug Rirmor
content:
Tags: city, ruin

A ruined city-state on the Xeltri Plateau, destroyed by hill giants.

TODO

name: Gulf of Joswik
content:
Tags: bay, sea, geography

A gulf near Joswik on Ofrenia.

TODO

name: Gulf of Kazül
content: The western coastline, where fishing villages and small ports connect Kazül to sea trade.

name: Gümloskos
content:
The northwestern peninsula of Konai Mora, jutting into the Ocean of Azuros north of Solhalven. Gümloskos is mountainous and wind-scoured, its peaks lower than the Blistering Mountains to the south but no less treacherous. This is the domain of Zosuun, the albino ice-fire dragon who has claimed the region since before recorded history.

The Valkyries maintain a careful neutrality with Zosuun. The dragon's hunting grounds overlap with their patrol routes, but neither party seems interested in conflict. Travelers seeking passage through Gümloskos are advised to move quickly and avoid camping in the open—Zosuun has been known to steal memories from those who linger in his territory.

name: Gwyrdyr Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Iylovia.

TODO

name: Gyerekas
content:
Tags: hills, geography

The southern boundary of the Dunes of Kunagi, where the sand sea breaks against a range of low, strange hills that aren't hills at all. Gyerekas is a fossilized reef—the remains of an ocean floor from an age when the desert was seafloor, now thrust up and exposed by geological forces older than human memory.

The Husakas call it the Bone Garden. They're not far wrong.

name: Gyosha
content:
Tags: island, geography

An island off the Seacleft Coast. Home to the city-state of Mirko.

name: Hagswood
content:
A dark forest at the southwest tip of Chaal Nazzerox, home to a coven of hags who terrorize the surrounding waters. The hags were here before the empire—they welcomed Xynoth's rise, seeing opportunity in chaos, and have carved out a domain within his domain.

The relationship between the lich king and the Hagswood coven is unclear. They're not his servants; hags answer to no one. But they're not his enemies either. Some arrangement exists—territorial, transactional, or simply mutual disinterest. The hags get their hunting grounds; Xynoth gets whatever the hags provide in exchange.

The forest itself is perpetually dim, its canopy blocking even midday sun. Paths shift between visits. Travelers who enter without hag permission rarely leave at all.

name: Hallenfell
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

Mountains on Apketrii.

TODO

name: Hana Mosa
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Avalon.

TODO

name: Hardshell Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography, ve, krell

A diagonal range running through the northern Krell Lands, the Hardshell Mountains are the insectoid heartland—the most densely populated Krell territory on Ve. Multiple queens have carved their hives into these peaks, their tunnels extending miles beneath the surface.

name: Hashigama
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Shyona.

TODO

name: Hellion Hills
content:
Tags: hills, wurmspine, ley lines, fire, geography

The Hellion Hills mark the northwestern terminus of the Wurmspine range, where the great mountain chain fragments into a chaotic jumble of volcanic peaks, smoking vents, and treacherous ravines. The fire ley line cuts directly through this region, and the land shows it—the hills run hot, the air tastes of sulfur, and the ground itself seems to simmer.

The name isn't metaphorical. These hills are hell to cross.

name: Helmsbridge
content:
The capital city, built where a major river crosses from the interior to the western coast. The bridge that gives the city its name is ancient—pre-dating the current kingdom by centuries—and wide enough for four wagons abreast. The royal palace sits on a bluff overlooking the bridge, positioned to watch everything that crosses.

The city is orderly, clean, and oppressively quiet. Citizens go about their business efficiently and speak to strangers only when necessary. The informant network that keeps Alisandra in power is headquartered here, though no building bears that label.

name: Hik
content:
Tags: citystate, free-isles, teflin

The city at the edge of the impossible, where exiles built a kingdom in the shadow of alien light. Hik sits on the western shore of Xabraedia, separated from the main Free Isles by The Spout and from normal reality by proximity to Starfall. The Velathi family—teflin outcasts who found refuge here generations ago—have adapted to the purple influence in ways that unsettle the other families. Their blood runs colors that aren't quite right, and their eyes see things they shouldn't.

name: Hillat Abu Hizam
content:
The capital of Aal Salma, an ancient port city built in concentric tiers above the cliffs of Prison Bay. Its name means "Belt-Town" in the old trade tongue—a reference to the defensive walls that ring the city like bands on a barrel, each built higher than the last as the city grew upward.

The lowest tier holds the harbor, shipyards, and warehouses. Middle tiers contain markets, guild halls, and the famous School of Charts—a cartographic academy whose maps command premium prices across Aboyinzu. The highest tier belongs to the old merchant families, whose compounds look down on everything below.

Hillat Abu Hizam has never been conquered. Its walls are strong, its harbor defensible, and its rulers have always been willing to pay tribute rather than fight—then outlast their would-be conquerors through patience and commerce. The current crisis in Poum Titch worries them. Wars are bad for trade, and desperate neighbors make dangerous ones.

name: Hills of Anaotki
content: A range of forested hills in northwestern Qet Yemani, marking the transition between the jungle lowlands and the western highlands. The hills are less densely vegetated than the deep jungle, with rocky outcrops and small clearings. They serve as a natural defensive barrier and contain several minor passes leading west.

name: Hills of Deoria
content:
Central highlands named for a kingdom that no longer exists. Deoria fell to Xynoth's first campaigns, its people scattered or enslaved, its cities reduced to ruins now picked over by the undead.

The Hills of Deoria are largely empty—depopulated by war and never resettled. Crumbling villages dot the slopes, their inhabitants long dead or fled. Cave systems riddle the rock, used by refugees hiding from undead patrols. The caves connect in ways the empire hasn't fully mapped, allowing the desperate to move unseen between regions.

Occasionally, someone emerges from the Hills of Deoria with pre-empire artifacts—jewelry, weapons, records of a nation that was. These fetch high prices among collectors willing to deal with goods from the undead lands.

name: Hills of Dolor
content:
Tags: hills, geography, region

The hills where the nameless come to die—or to begin again.

The Hills of Dolor are a barren expanse of rocky ridges and sun-scorched valleys in north-central Ve, wedged between the Dunes of Evioli to the east and the plains of Sestros to the west. The name translates from old Shyonan as "Hills of Grief," and for most of Ve's history, that grief was literal: these hills were where Shyona sent its exiles to perish.

name: Hills of Gezzeri
content:
Tags: poi

Mad max, water starved people living in the hills/cliffs of the desert. Also have to hide from the giants by disappearing into holes in the cliffy hills.

Home to the Gezzerin

name: Hills of Gray Stone
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills within Shyona.

TODO

name: Hills of Red Gold
content: The southern slopes, where most mining occurs. Rich veins of gold give the hills their name—and their value. Etherweaver goblins work the mines constantly, extracting wealth for the dragon's hoard.

name: Hills of Sharp Talons
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in Wycendeula.

TODO

name: Hills of Soromun
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in Lier, Erpeus.

TODO

name: Hills to Nowhere
content:
Tags: hills, wurmspine, geography, wasteland

The Hills to Nowhere occupy the southern reaches of the Wurmspine range, a desolate highland region stretching from Kobuk in the north to the arid wastes beyond. The name is accurate: the hills lead nowhere anyone wants to go. No valuable passes, no important settlements, no strategic advantage. Just empty terrain that serves primarily to be crossed—or avoided entirely.

name: Honor River
content:
Tags: river, geography, ve, avalon

The central waterway of Avalon, flowing from the eastern highlands westward through the kingdom's heart before emptying into Munari Inlet. The Honor River valley contains Avalon's best farmland, its capital city, and most of its population.

name: Howlwood
content:
Tags: forest, geography, edari, ve

A dense forest sprawling across northeastern Edari, where the golden savannah gives way to thick woodland near the coast of Coquray Sound. The forest earned its name from the eerie howling that echoes through the trees at night—a sound that carries for miles across the surrounding grassland.

name: Howlwood
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest in Iylovia.

TODO

name: Hume Une
content:
Ruins of a Kor colony on the largest of the Estri Islands. Centuries ago, the Kor—the "civilized" Koren—attempted to establish a foothold on Ve, building a walled settlement in the traditional Kor style: orderly streets, stone structures, a central hall for social rituals. The colony failed within two generations. Disease, supply problems, and increasingly hostile relations with the mainland Korel made the position untenable. The survivors evacuated by ship; the settlement was left to rot.

The Korel don't fear Hume Une—they find it disgusting. The ruins represent everything they rejected: walls, permanence, suppression of instinct, the Kor obsession with propriety. Entering the ruins is taboo, an act of spiritual contamination. Young Korel sometimes dare each other to approach the islands; actually setting foot on them would mean exile from the pack.

The ruins themselves are overgrown but largely intact. Stone doesn't rot. The central hall still stands, its interior dark and silent. Whatever the Kor left behind when they fled—records, artifacts, supplies—remains untouched. No Korel will retrieve it. No outsider has bothered to look.

name: Husakas
content:
Tags: desert, geography, culture

The southwestern reaches of the Dunes of Kunagi, where the sand sea breaks against the ancient fossil hills of Gyerekas and thins toward the Spotted Forest. Husakas is both a place and a people—the scorpion riders who have claimed this unforgiving territory as home for longer than anyone can remember.

Which is fitting, given what the land does to memory.

name: Iceburn Lakes
content: TODO

name: Iirmos
content:
Once the capital of Zelidia, Iirmos now stands empty—a wound in the jungle where the canopy refuses to close. The city's bone-white spires still rise above the treeline, visible for miles, but no Zelle has set foot there in centuries.

The High Priestesses do not speak of what happened. The jungle remembers.

What is known: the priestesses of Iirmos attempted a great blood-working, a ritual to permanently ward Zelidia from all outsiders. They sacrificed not individuals but entire villages of captives, channeling more blood than had ever been offered. Something answered—but it was not the jungle-spirits they knew.

The ritual tore open a passage to somewhere else. What came through consumed Iirmos in a single night. The survivors fled to what is now Glezamos, and the High Priestesses sealed the city behind wards of a different kind: wards meant not to keep things out, but to keep something in.

The jungle has grown strange around Iirmos. Trees bend away from the ruins. Animals fall silent within a mile of its walls. Those who approach report hearing voices—not screaming, but welcoming. Inviting them inside. Promising they'll never need to leave.

name: Inside Passage
content:
The sheltered waterway between Roule and the western islands, including Nylos. The Inside Passage offers calm sailing and protection from open-ocean storms, making it the preferred route for coastal traders and fishing fleets.

Roule controls the Passage absolutely. Naval checkpoints at both ends inspect vessels, collect tariffs, and turn away anyone deemed undesirable. Nylos fishing boats must obtain Roulean permits. Smugglers use hidden coves and night sailing, but the penalties for getting caught are severe.

name: Iqes Sound
content:
Tags: sea, geography, trade

The body of water south of Iqes, between the island and the Upoceax mainland. Unlike the treacherous Ripmaw Sound to the west or the ship-killing waters around the Golem's Teeth to the east, Iqes Sound is relatively safe — the main artery for legitimate trade flowing to and from Mjiqa.

The sound is broad, deep, and well-charted. Merchant vessels from across the Seacleft Coast pass through daily, carrying goods to Iqes's markets and returning laden with exports. The waters are busy enough that pirates rarely operate here openly; too many witnesses, too many potential rescuers, too much risk of encountering an Iqes privateer who'd rather eliminate competition than share hunting grounds.

name: Iron Academy
content:
Tags: poi, school

The greatest combat school in Alaria, and the de facto capital of Knova. The Academy isn't just located in Knova—it IS Knova. The institution and the nation are one and the same.

name: Isle of the Night
content: West of the mainland, situated in the waters between Moigos and the Tyvern Isles, lies the Isle of the Night — a mysterious island enveloped within the Gloomterror Forest.

name: Ismail (Port City)
content:
Where the Honor River empties into Munari Inlet stands Ismail, Avalon's window to the world. The city of perhaps 12,000 serves as the kingdom's primary port—nearly everything that enters or leaves Avalon by sea passes through Ismail's harbor.

The city sits at a natural chokepoint. The Honor River narrows as it approaches the coast, and Ismail controls both banks. Warehouses line the waterfront, storing goods moving between river barges and oceangoing vessels. The textile trade dominates—bolts of Goldwood-dyed cloth, time-touched ritual implements, and agricultural products flow outward, while metal goods, exotic materials, and foreign luxuries flow in.

Ismail's character differs from inland Avalon. The constant flow of foreigners—sailors, merchants, travelers—dilutes the theocracy's grip. The priests maintain temples and enforce the major observances, but Ismail's residents are more worldly than their rural cousins. Declarations still govern local children's lives, but visitors operate under looser rules, and the commerce-minded locals have learned to accommodate difference.

The harbor master, always a priest of high standing, balances religious duty against commercial necessity. Ships carrying forbidden goods (certain magical items, materials the Ecclesiarch has proscribed) are turned away. Ships carrying merely unusual cargo are taxed and monitored. The Ecclesiarch presumably foresees what passes through his port; the harbor master acts accordingly.

name: Iypos
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

The dominant city-state on the island of Migos—a trading hub built on the lower slopes of Mount Iypos, controlling the only significant harbor on the largest island in the northern Western Isles.

name: Jackal Mountains
content: The **Jackal Mountains** form the central spine of the Dalizi Highlands, a vast wall of stone that separates the eastern ranges from the western approaches to the Dalizi Confederation. Named for the packs of dire jackals that hunt the lower slopes, the mountains are the largest single range in the highlands and the primary reason no roads cross from east to west.

name: Jahazai Desert
content: The vast interior desert. Harsh, beautiful, deadly. Ancient ruins lie buried beneath the sands—remnants of civilizations that existed before the desert claimed the land. Treasure hunters venture in; many don't return.

name: Jeh Bli
content:
Tags: city, state, orc, farlands

A Xi'ivrach orc city-state on the shores of Lake Kro Nymos, in the remote northern Farlands. The only permanent settlement for hundreds of miles in any direction.

See [Jeh Bli (State)](../../nations_and_powers/states/Jeh%20Bli.md) for political details.

name: Jiji River
content:
Tags: river, geography

A river in Nashua.

TODO

name: Jinxz Plains
content:
Tags: plains, region

The Jinxz Plains occupy the southern stretch of the central grasslands, named (according to the goblins) for the bad luck that follows anyone who crosses them wrong. Like the Shiverplains to the north, this is Scalawag goblin territory—chaotic, dangerous, and perpetually at war with itself.

name: Jowdo
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Jüt
content:
Tags: city, state, port, geography

A port city of roughly twenty thousand people positioned on Kettle Bay, where the Westwilds coast meets the sea. Jüt is the region's window to the outside world—the only significant harbor on this stretch of coastline, and the place where the Westwilds' strange goods reach the broader markets.

name: Kagoro
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kair
content:
The capital city, built around a natural harbor on Elessi Bay. Kair's architecture follows Eloweir tradition—curved lines, circular plazas, buildings that flow into each other like water. The city feels organic, grown rather than constructed, though the engineering behind that appearance is sophisticated.

The council chambers occupy a headland overlooking the harbor, a reminder that in Eloesi, political power answers to the sea. Markets sprawl along the waterfront, trading pearls, fish, poisons, and goods from across the Middle Sea.

name: Kalemeydani
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in the United Free Nations.

TODO

name: Kampaga
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kanguka
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kapsayi
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Karahoroz
content: Avalon's eastern city, Karahoroz sits where the Honor River approaches Dern. It serves as administrative center for the eastern fiefdoms and as a trading hub with the mountain states. The city's priests maintain close ties with Dern—the mountain kingdom has its own traditions, but respects Avalon's theocracy enough to cooperate on border matters.

name: Kargak
content:
The capital city covers most of the main island's northern coast. Built by a people desperate to stay dry, Kargak is an architectural marvel of paranoia.

**Notable Features:**

- **The Dry Palace**: Central complex where the nobility conducts all business. Elaborate drainage, covered walkways, and sealed chambers keep seawater permanently excluded. The Tasting ceremonies occur in specially designed pools that can be drained and refilled with imported freshwater.

- **The Covered Streets**: During monsoon season, fabric awnings extend across every street in the noble quarter. Commoners walk in the rain; nobles never feel a drop.

- **The Piers of Exchange**: The waterfront where trade occurs. Nobles negotiate from covered platforms; goods are transferred by commoners who can afford to get wet. Foreign merchants find the arrangement baffling.

- **The House Compounds**: Each noble house maintains a sealed estate. Some haven't allowed outside water within their walls in generations.

name: Karik Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills within the Grendenheim region.

TODO

A location in Kafula.

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Kerikos

name: Kettle Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography

A sheltered natural harbor at the base of the Cliffs of Shondromos—calm waters surrounded by honeycombed cliffs riddled with caves. An excellent anchorage if you don't mind what might be watching from the cave mouths.

name: Khurgril
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

TODO

name: Kibuhanda
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kidwood
content:
The southern forest, between the Aphenlein system and the Dalizi Highlands. Kidwood is less dramatic than the northern regions—standard rainforest without the flooding of Aphenlein or the hills of Seyiy—but it serves as a transition zone where the Elder Wilds begin to give way to the different character of the Dalizi.

The Frosnos River flows through Kidwood, one of the primary waterways connecting the interior to the southern coast. Small Shemazari ruins dot the forest, but none of the major pyramid sites.

---

name: Killing Hills
content:
The southern highlands between the Aktarvkus and Mythlias, where the mountains break down into a chaotic landscape of steep ridges and narrow valleys. The name is earned: these hills have killed more travelers, soldiers, and would-be settlers than any other region of Kyagos.

The Kuru beast-warriors treat the Killing Hills as sacred ground. Their warriors come here to prove themselves, hunting each other and anything else that enters. Surviving a solo journey through the Killing Hills marks a Kuru as an adult; dying there is considered an honorable fate.

For everyone else, the Killing Hills are simply an obstacle—one of several reasons why overland travel between Tekos and the western city-states rarely happens. Better to take a ship around the coast, despite the dangers, than to cross terrain where everything is trying to kill you.

name: Kmokosiri
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest within Shyona.

TODO

name: Koilos
content:
The forest covering southern Ios, stretching from the Dipindre valley to the coast. Koilos is dense and largely unexplored — the stone dolls never worked there (they were designed for agriculture and construction, not forestry), and Ios never developed a timber industry.

The forest is rumored to hold ruins from before the current settlement, structures that predate even Todakres. Whether these are remnants of an earlier civilization or something else entirely, no one knows. Few Ios citizens venture into Koilos, and those who do don't always return.

name: Koilos Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Koisokamba
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kokotintin
content:
Tags: city, state, nation, free-isles

The jewel of the Free Isles and the wealthiest city in southern Alaria. Kokotintin is a sprawling metropolis of canals, marble palaces, and blood-soaked arenas where fortunes change hands faster than the tides. It is the seat of the Valdrossi family—the most powerful of the Five Families—and the undisputed commercial capital of the archipelago.

name: Koldkin Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in the Dragon's Spine Coast.

TODO

name: Kregkard
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

TODO

name: Kumi Wood
content:
North of Umlas, Kumi Wood stretches from the foothills of the Pearly Mountains to the coast near the Bay of Lords. This ancient hardwood forest is sacred to both Petranos and Kethros—the earth that grows the trees and the craft that shapes them.

Every tree felled in Kumi Wood requires ritual permission. Foresters must petition the local temple, specifying which tree, for what purpose, and offering appropriate thanks to Petranos for the gift. This sounds like bureaucratic obstruction, but it actually ensures sustainable harvesting. Kumi Wood has been producing exceptional timber for centuries and shows no sign of depletion.

The wood itself is prized across Alaria. Kumi hardwood takes stain beautifully, resists rot, and—when worked by Xothic craftsmen invoking Kethros—holds minor enchantments well. Instruments made from Kumi wood are particularly valued; some say they never go out of tune.

Settlements in Kumi Wood tend to be small and focused on forestry or woodcraft. Ku'or, the largest, serves as the administrative center for lumber operations and hosts a major temple to Kethros.

name: Kunagi Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

A transitional range of scrub-covered ridges and rocky outcrops marking the boundary between Atriik's settled lands and the Dunes of Kunagi proper. The hills run roughly north-south along Atriik's eastern edge, providing a natural barrier against the desert's encroachment—and a last opportunity for water before the true sands begin.

name: Kyedibugyo
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Kyorda
content: A small state on the southern coast of the Frostmarch Peninsula, clinging to the harsh shoreline where the peninsula meets the Denrik Sea.

name: Lacire
content:
Tags: city, state, geography

A city of perhaps fifteen thousand people built on and around the Springs of Vyowehr, deep within Ierya at the heart of the Walking Forest. Lacire is the only significant permanent settlement in the entire Walking Forest—and it exists because its people have learned to live in silence.

name: Lake Shikaya
content: The lake still exists, now surrounded by ruins rather than palatial estates. Drasnian refugees have established camps along its shores, using the water access as a foundation for their attempts at rebuilding something functional. The lake's fish provide food; its position provides defensibility. If any organized settlement emerges from Syvlius's chaos, it will probably center here.

name: Larsoia Lake
content: A larger lake in the central Grognil, partially fed by underground springs that keep sections from freezing completely. The water is drinkable and the shores occasionally show signs of old camps, but no permanent settlement has ever taken hold. Travelers who stay more than a few nights report increasingly vivid dreams of drowning—not in water, but in absolute stillness, unable to move or cry out.

name: Lean D'Kas
content:
The northwestern section, named for reasons lost. "Kas" might have been a Riin leader, a spirit, or a word whose meaning died with the language. Lean D'Kas is less dense than Lean D'Riin, less obviously malevolent, but still not safe.

The Riin settled here more lightly—seasonal camps rather than permanent cities. The ruins are smaller, more scattered, easier to miss. This makes Lean D'Kas more dangerous in some ways: travelers don't realize they've crossed into forbidden territory until the forest starts responding.

name: Lean D'Riin
content:
Tags: forest, geography, riin, eyendra

The central and most ancient section of the Eyendra forest. The name means "Forest of the Riin" in the language of the civilization that once flourished here—a people who merged with the land two thousand years ago and now exist only as a diffuse presence throughout the forest.

Lean D'Riin is the heart of ancient Riin civilization and the most dangerous part of Eyendra. The Riin built (grew) their largest settlements here, and here the merger between people and land was most complete. The trees are impossibly old—some predate the Riin civilization itself—and the canopy is so thick that the forest floor exists in permanent twilight.

No one lives in Lean D'Riin. No one should go there unless they have business with the dragon Ezra Olkanis or wish to disappear.

See the main **Eyendra** entry for full details on the forest, the Riin, and the dangers of the interior.

name: Lekia
content:
The cultural co-capital, a coastal city on Whitefin Bay famous for its amphitheaters, festival grounds, and the Philosopher's Wharf—a long stone pier where Scalawag thinkers hold public debates, sometimes for days on end, drawing crowds who cheer arguments like sporting matches.

Lekia's harbor shelters fishing fleets and the ornate festival barges that carry revelers between coastal celebrations. The city's architecture favors open plazas and tiered seating, designed for performance and spectacle. Even the fish markets have stages.

The war has changed everything. Rebel-sympathizing ships blockade the harbor, strangling trade. The festival grounds have become refugee camps. The philosophers still debate on the Wharf, but now they argue about who deserves to die.

name: Lemru Nymor
content:
The southeastern hills, between Kidwood and the coast. Lemru Nymor represents the last of the Elder Wilds before the terrain rises toward the Dalizi Highlands. The forest here is thinner, the hills more pronounced, the climate drier.

Anchor Trees are rare in Lemru Nymor—only a handful stand in the hills, suggesting that fewer titans died or were buried in this region.

---

name: Levke
content:
The capital, carved into the heart of the central Thunderdark range. Levke is less a city than a mountain made habitable—halls cut deep into living rock, forges burning perpetually in the depths, and defensive positions that have never been breached.

The **Hall of Glorious Ends** dominates Levke's center, a vast chamber where the names and deeds of honored dead are carved into the walls. Every Strømgodden aspires to have their death recorded here—not their life, their *death*, because how one dies defines one's worth. The most spectacular, most sacrificial, most glorious endings receive the most elaborate carvings.

The **High Forge** of Levke produces the finest spiked armor in Trømgar, crafted by master smiths who have spent decades perfecting the art. A suit of Levke armor is a status symbol, a sacred object, and a lethal weapon all in one.

The ruling **Warcouncil** meets in Levke's deepest chambers, where decisions affecting all Trømgar are made by the most battle-proven leaders.

name: Lier
content:
Tags: state, nation

Lier occupies the central portion of Erpeus Island, sandwiched between the gnomish mountains of Mkinti to the south and the monastic peaks of Azuros to the north. It's the human heartland of Erpeus—rolling hills, fertile valleys, and a pragmatic population that's learned to profit from its position as middleman between the secretive gnomes and the outside world.

name: Lier - Erpeus Island
content: Lier is a coastal state within the Middle Sea region, home to the main port settlement of Hessam on Erpeus Island.

name: Linas
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state on the Seacleft Coast.

TODO

name: Lise
content:
Tags: city, settlement

The capital of Enapay and seat of the Dragonbound Queen. Lise sprawls where the Ishtar River delta meets the Green Wilds, a city built to accommodate both its Dwelyn inhabitants and their ancient dragon protector.

name: Lizard's Maw
content:
A distinctive inlet on the southwestern coast, where the Beastlands meet the Gulf of Chimea. From above, the coastline forms a shape resembling an open reptilian jaw—a curved peninsula forming the upper palate, a sandbar the lower jaw, and a narrow channel between them like a throat.

The Ix'Meglyakuk consider Lizard's Maw sacred. Their creation myths hold that the Beastlands themselves are the body of a primordial lizard-god, sleeping beneath the jungle, and the Maw is where the god will speak when it finally wakes. Tribal gatherings happen on the shores during certain astronomical alignments, and outsiders who approach during these ceremonies are killed without warning.

At other times, the Maw serves as the primary point of contact between the Ix'Meglyakuk and Chimea. The channel is navigable for shallow-draft vessels, and a Chimean trading post—Fort Vaylen—has survived on the outer peninsula for over sixty years, protected by treaties negotiated with the nearest tribes and by its location outside the sacred boundaries.

**Fort Vaylen:** The only permanent non-Ix'Meglyakuk settlement in the Beastlands, Fort Vaylen is a stockaded compound housing perhaps a hundred Chimean traders, factors, and guards. They purchase jungle products from willing tribes and sell them upriver to Chimea proper. The fort's survival depends entirely on maintaining good relations with local Ix'Meglyakuk—when those relations sour, as they did in 3,298 SD, the jungle closes in fast. That incident left forty Chimeans dead and Fort Vaylen abandoned for a decade before new treaties were negotiated.

name: Locquine
content:
Tags: citystate, ve, pixie, fae

A city-state deep in the jungles of southern Ve, ruled by pixies whose perception of reality has fundamentally diverged from that of other races. Outsiders call them insane. The Locquine pixies call everyone else blind.

name: Lodrian Hills
content:
Tags: hills, geography

Hills in Old Tolaria.

TODO

name: Lucenia River
content:
Tags: river, geography

A river in Gorath.

TODO

name: Lupuno
content:
Tags: city, state

A Kor city-state in northwestern Atriik, built where the Sha Puniki emerges from the Milras Forest. Lupuno controls the timber trade and river commerce for the region.

See [Atriik](Atriik.md#lupuno) for full details.

name: Lutuya Sound
content:
A southwestern bay where the empire's attempted shipbuilding efforts have repeatedly failed. Xynoth needs a navy to break free of his geographic containment, and Lutuya Sound has the deep water and sheltered anchorage to support one. The problem is the hags.

Every shipyard established in Lutuya Sound has been sabotaged, burned, or simply emptied of workers who vanished in the night. The hags apparently prefer their hunting grounds undisturbed by imperial naval traffic. Xynoth could force the issue—commit real resources to clearing Hagswood—but so far he's chosen to focus elsewhere. The standoff continues.

---

name: Luyuge
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: M'Syvla
content:
Tags: state, nation

A state in Central Aboyinzu.

TODO

name: Magayi
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Magma Pits
content:
Tags: ley lines, fire, earth, volcanic, geography, dangerous

The Magma Pits mark the convergence of Alaria's fire and earth ley lines at the eastern edge of Zlyverth Korn, where the Twaan Forests meet the foothills of the Kharvorn Mountains. This is one of the most powerful elemental convergences on the continent—a place where raw magical force has reshaped the land itself.

name: Majesty Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains

Home to numerous Kimril, copious quantities of red gold, and the an infamous dwarf bandit. 

Used to be homeland of stone dwarves, but it was destroyed by dragonriders from Adron. All of the underground cities and ruins of the stone dwarves are now infested with goblins and other nasties that the Adron government has given up trying to eradicate.

They removed the dwarves by just flaming the tunnels with their dragons, but now they don't really have the same dragon population they used to, and they can't ever quite get the last of the goblins, which is all it takes for them all to immediately repopulate the mountains.

name: Makuri
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Marjansi
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Marrow Valley
content: The **Marrow Valley** cuts through the southwestern Foggy Mountains, a narrow defile that connects the Elder Mountains to the central highlands. The valley takes its name from Marrow Creek, which runs red with iron-rich sediment during spring melt—and from the bones that occasionally wash down with the seasonal floods.

name: Maruro
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Maruzi
content:
A city-state in Dalizi.

TODO

name: Masaad Takid
content:
A broad, sheltered bay or inland sea on Aal Salma's northern coast—warm, shallow, and rich with life. Pearl divers work its sandy bottoms. Fishing boats crisscross its calm waters. Trade ships move between Zamrat Aqabah and smaller coastal towns without risking the open ocean.

The bay's inlets and coves have always sheltered smugglers and, occasionally, pirates. Aal Salma's navy patrols when it can, but Masaad Takid has too many hiding places to control completely. The authorities mostly focus on keeping the main shipping lanes clear and let the margins police themselves.

name: Masadi
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Mathavia Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

A north-south mountain range forming the boundary between the elven territories of the Farlands and the Giant Lands to the east. The Mathavia Mountains are old and weathered, their peaks lower than the Drystone range but still formidable barriers to travel. Dense forests cover the western slopes (within elven territory), while the eastern slopes descend into the scrublands bordering the Xeltri Plateau and Malo Kon Greikh.

name: Maun
content:
A dwarven city-state on the southern coast of SW Urok, famous for its School of Music and Theater. Maun sits where the Maun River empties into Beauty Bay, its harbor busy with ships carrying students, graduates, and the instruments they craft. See the full state entry for details on its martial traditions, mandatory service requirements, and the mysterious Ghost who haunts its halls.

The city is as much heard as seen—music drifts from every quarter, students practicing at all hours, the sounds of a hundred instruments blending into a constant, ever-shifting symphony. Visitors either fall in love with Maun or flee within a day. There is no middle ground.

name: Maurevelious
content:
The capital of Phirexes rises from the swamp on stilts and floating platforms, a city that seems grown rather than built. Queen Phendexelas constructed it in a single impossible year after her arrival, and the architecture reflects her alien aesthetics: organic curves, living wood that still grows and must be trimmed like hedges, windows of smoky glass that seem to watch passersby. The streets are raised boardwalks connecting platform-neighborhoods, and beneath them the swamp water laps eternally at the foundations.

Thirty thousand Dengar live here, the vast majority of Phirexes' population. They are not slaves—they own businesses, raise families, practice their ancestral craft of swamp alchemy. But they are not free either. The Fog Gates at each city entrance "recognize" those who have lived here long enough; attempting to leave triggers the fog-binding sickness. And every evening, without exception, they line the Queen's Walk to prostrate themselves before their savior.

The Dengar's cultural philosophy—"the worse it gets, the better it was"—has curdled here into something like mass Stockholm syndrome. They survived the catastrophe, so this must be good. They have homes and work, so they should be grateful. The worse life was before the queen saved them, the better life is now by comparison. This self-gaslighting is the queen's most insidious weapon; she barely needs to oppress them when their own culture does it for her.

name: Melaia
content:
Tags: citystate

One of the three citystates making up the First Brotherhood. Home to dwarves.

name: Melodia
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A forest on the southern edge of the Piktiniti Desert, spilling down toward the coast where the land becomes less arid. Melodia is named for its most distinctive quality: the trees sing.

name: Merrow's Keep
content:
On the western slopes of the Aqueandrekons, overlooking Sorrow Lake, stand the ruins of Merrow's Keep. The fortress was built centuries ago by a lord whose name has been deliberately erased from all records—a punishment reserved for crimes so severe that even remembrance is forbidden.

The keep itself is intact but abandoned. Local legend claims that something the nameless lord summoned or created still inhabits the fortress, though no one has verified this for generations. Sorrow Lake below the keep is unnaturally still and deep, its waters dark enough that the bottom has never been seen.

Fishermen from the valleys to the west avoid Sorrow Lake entirely. They say the fish there taste wrong, and that things sometimes surface at night that shouldn't exist in freshwater.

name: Mgangugu
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Mighty Mountains
content:
Tags: mountains, geography

TODO

name: Migos
content:
Tags: island, geography

The largest single island in the northern Western Isles—a mountainous landmass that rises dramatically from Phyndarr Sound. Home to the city-state of Iypos and a population of independent-minded mountain folk who've never been successfully conquered.

name: Milras Forest
content:
Tags: forest, geography

A dense old-growth forest in southwestern Westrim, forming the northern boundary of Atriik. The Milras stretches from the Wyrm Teeth hills in the northeast toward Shark Bay in the west, separating the Atriik city-states from Kura and the northern desert margins.

The forest is ancient and deep—not as supernaturally disorienting as its distant cousin Milrar in the Westwilds, but old enough to discourage casual exploration. The Sha Puniki river flows from its eastern edge past Lupuno, and the timber trade from the forest's margins supports much of northwestern Atriik's economy.

name: Mirko
content:
A city-state on Gyosha Island.

TODO

name: Mirror Lakes
content:
Tags: lake, geography, wycendeula

A cluster of small lakes in southwestern Wycendeula, tucked into the hills where the Neurian range meets the northwestern edge of Eberri Ygonzi. Unlike the predator-infested waters of Kama Sa'malina or the psychological horror of Trauma Lake, the Mirror Lakes are merely... strange.

name: Miruyogoro
content: A city-state in Dalizi.

name: Misery Bay
content:
Tags: bay, geography

A bay in the Dragon's Spine Coast.

TODO

name: Mjiqa
content:
The capital of Iqes, situated at the mouth of the Shulka River. Mjiqa is where the coral-and-mica architecture reaches its most dramatic expression — towers of cultivated pink coral, walls that shimmer with mica flakes, broad plazas where the wealthy display their status in imported fashions and ostentatious jewelry.

Look past the glitter and you see the machinery of power: the Ring Hall where the council meets, the Coral Docks where trade flows, the Privateers' Berths where licensed raiders spend their earnings. And in the crowded Qindo Quarter, the workers who make it all function — dockworkers, servants, sailors, craftspeople whose labor builds the wealth they'll never share.

Mjiqa is two cities in one. The ring-holders see a jewel of commerce and culture. The Qindo see a stolen inheritance.

name: Mkinti
content:
Tags: state, nation

Mkinti occupies the southwestern portion of Erpeus Island, a land of glittering mountains and labyrinthine underground cities. This is Ohblex territory—gem gnomes who were born from the precious stones themselves and who guard the secret of clockwork jealously. The surface is all jagged peaks and treacherous passes; the real civilization lies beneath, in tunnels that sparkle with embedded gems and hum with mechanical precision.

name: Moonwood
content:
The great northern forest of Kyagos, stretching from the Sea of Seven Snakes coast inland to the mountain foothills. Dense canopy, ancient trees, and undergrowth thick enough to swallow armies. The Sylthik city-states never successfully colonized Moonwood—every attempt ended in disaster, ambush, or simple disappearance.

This is Dead Moon Tribe territory.

name: Mordon Basin
content:
A wide, flat basin in the western reaches of Argysis, once home to the famed Mordon vineyards. The dwarves of Argysis adapted traditional winemaking to alpine conditions, producing a pale, sharp vintage called *frostbite wine* that was prized throughout the Kharvorn region and beyond.

Rothogomos burned the vineyards during the conquest. The terraced hillsides remain, overgrown with wild grapes that no one harvests. A few desperate souls occasionally try to reclaim a patch—make wine, sell it to smugglers, buy passage out of Argysis. Most are caught and made examples of. Some aren't.

The basin's strategic importance outweighs its agricultural value. Mordon is the widest flat ground in Argysis, the only place where a large army could muster or a dragon could land without folding his wings awkwardly. Rothogomos keeps it deliberately empty for this reason. If war comes with Anarak, Mordon Basin will be the staging ground.

name: Morgnor's Cradle
content:
Tags: state, nation

The domain of Morgnor, an ancient earth-attuned dragon who rules over Etherweaver goblins in the Wurmspine Mountains northwest of Thespia. The goblins mine precious metals and enchant them for the dragon's ever-growing hoard.

Very rich. Very greedy. Very dangerous.

name: Morgrad
content:
Tags: city, state, nation

A city-state in Malo Kon Greikh.

TODO

name: Morygun Mountains
content:
A major mountain range running northwest to southeast, connecting the southern tip of the Crystal Mountains to the northwestern edge of Dragon's Spine. West of the Morygun Mountains lies uncharted wilderness—grasslands, forests, and the Korgül region. The southern reaches of the Morygun Mountains connect to the Remakemizi Mountains north of Kadroka state.
