Winzy Strait sees little traffic. No major trade routes pass through it; the waters lead to regions that generate neither significant exports nor demand for imports. Ships that use the strait are typically local fishing vessels, Tuktuk boats moving between their islands, or the occasional traveler who specifically wants to reach somewhere that most people don't go.

name: Wisewatch
content:
At the headwaters of Wisdom Creek, overlooking the ruins of Othyndria from a safe distance, stands Wisewatch—a small fortification that predates even the Argysisean kingdom. The watchtower was built by an unknown people to observe the spring that would later become Othyndria's heart.

Now Wisewatch serves as a gathering point for those who oppose Rothogomos—scholars studying the old kingdom, Hephakean agents, dwarven resistance fighters, and mercenaries seeking to loot the ruins. The dragon knows of their presence but seems to find them amusing rather than threatening. So far.

name: Wister Valley
content:
East of Xi Chin, the mountains open into Wister Valley—a high bowl of terraced farms and scattered villages that feeds the Timekeeper city. The valley's volcanic soil grows grain, vegetables, and the famous Wister apples, small and tart, that ferment into a cider prized across the Greenwater Isles.

But Wister Valley serves more than agriculture. Its orientation creates a natural corridor for observing specific star-rises, and ancient standing stones—predating Xi Chin itself—mark astronomical positions that the Chroniclers still use for their most precise calculations. Some scholars believe Xi Chin was built where it was specifically because of Wister Valley's sight-lines.

The valley folk are a mix of humans and dwarves whose families have worked this land for generations. They're proud, practical, and suspicious of the marble-city intellectuals who depend on their harvests but rarely deign to visit. "Xi Chin counts the stars," goes a local saying, "but Wister counts the sacks of grain."

name: Wizzik
content:
The current capital, seat of House Wizzik and now the center of Mkinti's government. Before the succession crisis, Wizzik was just one of five house seats; now it's everything.

The city descends through multiple underground levels, each more elaborate than the last. The deepest levels house the most sensitive workshops—where clockwork secrets are kept, where the finest gems are processed, where House Wizzik's inner circle schemes and plots.

The city is trapped. Every corridor, every doorway, every staircase has mechanisms that can seal, collapse, or kill. The gnomes navigate by memory and subtle markers; visitors navigate by luck and gnomish patience.

name: Wurm Tooth Mountain
content:
Tags: mountain, geography

TODO

name: Wurnfleur (Town)
content:
Population: ~700

A coastal town south of Svedlind, serving as Klevnaf's primary connection to the sea. Wurnfleur's harbor is better protected than Tangiern's rocky coast, and in peacetime the town would thrive on fishing and trade. The civil war has reduced traffic to a trickle—no merchant wants to be caught between elven factions.

The town maintains a small fleet of war-galleys, crewed by elves trained in naval combat. If Istora ever threatens from the sea, Wurnfleur would be the first defense.

name: Xyrionn
content:
In the hills at the center of Lethos stands a tree stump the size of a castle.

The tree that once grew here must have been ancient beyond reckoning—the stump is a quarter-mile across, and the wood, though dead, refuses to rot. Paths wind through the exposed root system, creating a natural maze that leads to the hollow center. At the heart of the maze lives a druid named Veleth.

Veleth is old. How old, no one knows—certainly older than the Binding, possibly older than Gorath itself. They tend the maze, grow things in the hollow center, and react with violent fury to anyone who attempts to harm the sacred wood. The stump is resistant to most forms of magic, as if the tree's death somehow concentrated its essence into impenetrability.

The Vigil meets here because Xyrionn is the heart of the Binding. The roots that spread from this stump connect to every part of Lethos, and the souls of the Gorathi dead are woven through them. Veleth doesn't control the Binding—no one does, not anymore—but they understand it better than anyone. When the villages have questions about the dead, about the jungle's behavior, about signs and portents, they come to Veleth.

The maze is not just difficult to navigate. The root-paths shift, especially at night, especially during Hollownight. Those who enter without permission find themselves walking the same paths forever, or emerging days later with no memory of the intervening time, or not emerging at all. The dead do not like visitors.

name: Yanayazi
content:
*"Water-That-Remembers-Fire"*

Southwestern Yanayazi sits where the Scheleur River runs closest to the Firespine before turning west toward the sea. The river runs warm here—not hot enough to harm, but noticeably above the temperature of normal water. The Neka of Yanayazi have built their lives around this peculiarity.

Yanayazi is the fishing village, though "fishing" understates what they do. The Scheleur's warmth supports species found nowhere else—heat-adapted fish, crustaceans that cluster around thermal vents, amphibians that bask in the warm shallows. Yanayazi fishers have developed specialized techniques for harvesting these creatures, some of which are valuable to outside alchemists and gourmands.

The village is also the confederacy's connection to the western interior. The Scheleur is navigable by small craft, and Yanayazi traders pole their way upriver to deal with other Urok peoples—carefully, given the jungle's dangers, but profitably enough to justify the risk. They're the most mercantile of the Neka, with a practical streak that sometimes frustrates the more traditional villages.

Yanayazi's relationship with the Firespine is complicated. They're close enough to feel its influence but not close enough to produce many fire-speakers. Instead, they've developed their own spiritual tradition around the warm river. They believe the Scheleur carries Yolu-Kazyn's blessing outward—that the heat in the water is the serpent's gift to lands too far from the spine to receive fire directly. River-prayers before fishing expeditions are common, and Yanayazi mourning rituals involve releasing the deceased's ashes into the current to be carried toward the sea.

**Hooks:**
- Yanayazi traders can arrange passage upriver for those with legitimate business—and sufficient payment
- The warm-water species occasionally include things that shouldn't exist
- Something upstream has been fouling the water, and the village wants to know what

name: Yava
content:
The largest city on Pesalolo, at the island's southwestern tip where the Ryujilin Hills meet the sea. Yava exists because everyone needs it to exist.

The city is the only major deepwater port between Screamstrait and the Slave Coast—a geographic necessity for any vessel crossing the Gindrik Sea. Ships from Ethadia, Shir Island, Windori, and points beyond require Yava for resupply, repair, and shelter from the storms that sweep through Screamstrait seasonally. Merchants from a dozen nations use the port as neutral ground.

This makes Yava untouchable.

Gorath could reach it. The imperial navy is more than capable of sailing around Pesalolo and burning the city to the waterline. But doing so would destabilize trade across the entire region—including Gorath's own legitimate commerce. Every major trading power has made clear, through diplomatic channels, that destroying Yava would have consequences.

So Gorath pretends Yava is a minor fishing town beneath imperial notice. Yava pretends it doesn't shelter escapees from the Slave Coast. Everyone maintains the fiction because the alternative hurts everyone's bottom line.

**Population**: Large by Tamadrezan standards—perhaps thirty thousand permanent residents, with thousands more transients at any given time. The majority are Drasnian dwarves, but foreign sailors, traders, and people who don't ask questions have made Yava genuinely cosmopolitan.

**Character**: Yava is not a resistance headquarters or a utopia. It is a grubby, pragmatic port city that survives by being useful to people who could destroy it. The docks smell of fish and tar. The taverns serve anyone with coin. The city watch takes bribes but maintains enough order to keep merchants coming back.

The closest thing Tamadrez has to a capital, though no one would call it that officially. When Tamadrezan leaders need to coordinate—which is rarely—they meet in Yava. When escaped slaves make it through the mountains and need to disappear, many end up here. When Gorathi deserters want a new life, Yava doesn't ask about their past.

The city faces southwest, toward Screamstrait and the wider world, deliberately turning its back on the Slave Coast hundreds of miles to the east. This orientation is architectural and philosophical. Yava wants nothing to do with Gorath's business—as long as Gorath leaves it alone.

name: Yift
content: The village closest to Glezamos, practically a suburb of the capital. Yift's artisans produce the material culture of Zelidian religion: ritual blades, wing-paints in sacred patterns, sacrificial implements, and the bone-chimes that hang throughout every Zelle settlement. Yift's workshops never fall silent.

name: Ylidor
content: An eastern town near the broken coastline. Known for its smoked fish and quiet contempt for whoever rules in Catalina.

name: Yorx
content: A frontier town at the edge of the Taflex Forest, serving as the gateway to Grimreach proper. Hunters, trappers, and monster-hunters use Yorx as a base of operations. The town maintains no permanent allegiance to any merchant-king, instead paying protection money to whichever house currently has forces in the area.

name: Yzre's Grave
content:
Tags: poi

Grave of an ord goldminer. Thought he'd find gold in the Falsehope Hills and spent his life's fortune on it. Found nothing, despite spending decades scouring the hills for the gold he was sure was there. Became bitter and never left, remaining as an upset Revenant, still searching for gold.

name: Zambullón
content:
A small village in the southern forest fringe, surviving on herbalism and logging. Zambullón guides are considered essential for anyone entering the Bolsaña P'Suelos—they know which patches of ground will support weight and which will swallow a traveler to the knees.

---

name: Zamrat Aqabah
content:
A northern port city on Masaad Takid, Aal Salma's second city and its moral opposite. Where Hillat Abu Hizam is regulated and respectable, Zamrat Aqabah is neither. Deals that can't be done in the capital get done here. Questions aren't asked. Paperwork gets lost.

The city's warehouses hold goods of uncertain provenance—salvage from wrecks that may not have been accidents, cargo that changed hands under duress, items that certain parties would prefer not be traced. The harbor masters take bribes openly. The city watch takes larger ones.

Since the Poum Titch civil war began, Zamrat Aqabah has swelled with Scalawag refugees. Some are genuine displaced persons; others are smugglers, spies, and war profiteers. The city's criminal economy has boomed. Hillat Abu Hizam pretends not to notice, as long as the tariff revenues keep flowing south.

---

name: Zetl Hesk Rou
content:
Tags: lake, giants-cradle, sacred-site, geography

"The Lake of Last Breath" in the giant tongue. A cold, impossibly deep lake in the western basin of Giants Cradle where giants come to die, walking into the dark water and never emerging.

name: Zetl Heski Voth
content:
Tags: lake, giants-cradle, sacred-site, geography

"The Lake of First Breath" in the giant tongue. A geothermally-heated lake in the eastern basin of Giants Cradle where giant tradition holds the first of their kind emerged from the earth.

name: Zubaydah
content: A settlement in the west, closer to the coast and the main trade routes to Kazül.

name: Zukisani
content:
The northern city on the Myusha River, controlling river trade from the northern forests and serving as Iqes's connection to Gyosha and the waters beyond. Less grand than Mjiqa, more practical — a working city where ships are built, goods are processed, and the fishing fleet operates.

Zukisani has a reputation for relatively honest dealing. The ring-holders here are merchants and shipwrights, not aristocrats playing political games. Qindo and foreign-descended residents mix more easily; the inequality remains, but the contempt is muted.

The shipyards of Zukisani are famous across the Seacleft Coast. The city also serves as the primary port for trade with Mirko and Gyosha — connections that bring both commerce and complications.

name: Zuko
content:
The southern interior city, controlling land routes through Iqes's forested heart. Zuko is where the ring-holders maintain their grip on the interior — tax collection, law enforcement, and military garrisons when the elite feel threatened by Ntando restlessness.

Less beautiful than Mjiqa, more fortified, Zuko is the fist inside Iqes's velvet glove. The garrison here is larger than a trading city needs; its purpose is control rather than defense. The Tax Houses process tribute from villages throughout the interior.

Ironically, Zuko also contains the best-organized resistance cells. The city that suppresses dissent is close enough to Ntando to feel its grievances, and not everyone in the garrison is loyal to the ring-holders.

Near Zuko lie the ruins of the largest halfling cave-complex — deliberately destroyed during the consolidation, now a scar on the landscape that halflings visit in quiet pilgrimage.

name: Zunuki
content:
The second city, traditionally controlled by one of the now-headless houses. Zunuki specialized in jewelry—combining Ohblex gem expertise with artistic design that fetched premium prices across the Middle Sea.

The city is in turmoil. House Wizzik has claimed control, but the local craftsmen are loyal to their dead masters. Production has slowed, quality has suffered, and everyone is waiting to see what happens next.

name: Zure
content: A small northern town serving the communities around the Smokestack region.

name: Zyquirk
content:
Tags: ruin

On Prophecy Island

When people settled on this isle, a prophet said that the isle would be destroyed by the gods. Nobody listened, but eventually the island erupted, and her prophecy came true. Now believed to be cursed.
